@[simp] theorem card_zero : Fintype.card (X 0) = 1 := by
  exact Fintype.card_unique (α := X 0)

@[simp] theorem endsInZero_zero (x : X 0) : EndsInZero x := by
  simp [EndsInZero, Omega.get]

@[simp] theorem not_endsInOne_zero (x : X 0) : ¬ EndsInOne x := by
  simp [EndsInOne, Omega.get]

discoveries.lean_statement: theorem endsInZero_or_endsInOne (x : X m) : EndsInZero x ∨ EndsInOne x
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  cases hBit : get x.1 (m - 1)
  · left
    simpa [EndsInZero] using hBit
  · right
    simpa [EndsInOne] using hBit

discoveries.lean_statement: theorem endsInZero_not_endsInOne (x : X m) (hZero : EndsInZero x) : ¬ EndsInOne x
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  intro hOne
  rw [EndsInZero] at hZero
  rw [EndsInOne] at hOne
  simp [hZero] at hOne

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem restrict_endsInZero_of_last_true (x : X (m + 1)) (hLast : Omega.last x.1 = true) :
    EndsInZero (restrict x)
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  dsimp [EndsInZero]
  cases m with
  | zero =>
      simp [Omega.get]
  | succ n =>
      have hLast' : get x.1 (n + 1) = true := by
        simpa [Omega.last, Omega.get] using hLast
      cases hPrev : get (restrict x).1 n with
      | false =>
          simpa [restrict, Omega.get] using hPrev
      | true =>
          have hPrevWord : get (Omega.truncate x.1) n = true := by
            simpa [restrict] using hPrev
          have hPrev' : get x.1 n = true := by
            rw [truncate_get_eq (w := x.1) (i := n) (h := Nat.lt_succ_self n)] at hPrevWord
            exact hPrevWord
          exact False.elim (x.2 n hPrev' hLast')

noncomputable def endsInCoverEquiv (m : Nat) :
    {x : X m // EndsInZero x ∨ EndsInOne x} ≃ X m where
  toFun x := x.1
  invFun x := ⟨x, endsInZero_or_endsInOne x⟩
  left_inv x := by
    cases x
    rfl
  right_inv x := rfl

noncomputable def endsInOrEquivSum (m : Nat) :
    {x : X m // EndsInZero x ∨ EndsInOne x} ≃
      ({x : X m // EndsInZero x} ⊕ {x : X m // EndsInOne x}) where
  toFun x := by
    classical
    by_cases hZero : EndsInZero x.1
    · exact Sum.inl ⟨x.1, hZero⟩
    · exact Sum.inr ⟨x.1, by
        rcases x.2 with hZero' | hOne
        · exact False.elim (hZero hZero')
        · exact hOne⟩
  invFun
    | Sum.inl x => ⟨x.1, Or.inl x.2⟩
    | Sum.inr x => ⟨x.1, Or.inr x.2⟩
  left_inv x := by
    classical
    rcases x with ⟨x, hx⟩
    by_cases hZero : EndsInZero x
    · simp [hZero]
    · have hOne : EndsInOne x := by
        rcases hx with hZero' | hOne
        · exact False.elim (hZero hZero')
        · exact hOne
      simp [hZero]
  right_inv x := by
    classical
    cases x with
    | inl x =>
        have hZero : EndsInZero x.1 := x.2
        simp [hZero]
    | inr x =>
        have hZero : ¬ EndsInZero x.1 := by
          intro hx
          exact endsInZero_not_endsInOne x.1 hx x.2
        simp [hZero]

noncomputable def appendFalseEquivLastFalse (m : Nat) :
    X m ≃ {x : X (m + 1) // Omega.last x.1 = false} where
  toFun x := ⟨appendFalse x, by simp [Omega.last]⟩
  invFun x := restrict x.1
  left_inv x := by
    simp
  right_inv x := by
    apply Subtype.ext
    simpa using appendFalse_reconstruct x.1 x.2

noncomputable def appendTrueEquivLastTrue (m : Nat) :
    {x : X m // EndsInZero x} ≃ {x : X (m + 1) // Omega.last x.1 = true} where
  toFun x := ⟨appendTrue x.1 x.2, by simp [Omega.last]⟩
  invFun x := ⟨restrict x.1, restrict_endsInZero_of_last_true x.1 x.2⟩
  left_inv x := by
    apply Subtype.ext
    simp
  right_inv x := by
    apply Subtype.ext
    simpa using appendTrue_reconstruct x.1 x.2 (restrict_endsInZero_of_last_true x.1 x.2)

@[simp] theorem card_endsInZero_succ (m : Nat) :
    Fintype.card {x : X (m + 1) // EndsInZero x} = Fintype.card (X m) := by
  classical
  simpa [EndsInZero, Omega.last, Omega.get] using
    (Fintype.card_congr (appendFalseEquivLastFalse m)).symm

@[simp] theorem card_endsInOne_succ (m : Nat) :
    Fintype.card {x : X (m + 1) // EndsInOne x} =
      Fintype.card {x : X m // EndsInZero x} := by
  classical
  simpa [EndsInOne, EndsInZero, Omega.last, Omega.get] using
    (Fintype.card_congr (appendTrueEquivLastTrue m)).symm

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem card_eq_endsInZero_add_endsInOne (m : Nat) :
    Fintype.card (X m) =
      Fintype.card {x : X m // EndsInZero x} +
      Fintype.card {x : X m // EndsInOne x}
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  classical
  calc
    Fintype.card (X m)
      = Fintype.card {x : X m // EndsInZero x ∨ EndsInOne x} := by
          simpa using (Fintype.card_congr (endsInCoverEquiv m)).symm
    _ = Fintype.card ({x : X m // EndsInZero x} ⊕ {x : X m // EndsInOne x}) := by
          exact Fintype.card_congr (endsInOrEquivSum m)
    _ = Fintype.card {x : X m // EndsInZero x} +
          Fintype.card {x : X m // EndsInOne x} := by
          simp [Fintype.card_sum]

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem card_recurrence (m : Nat) :
    Fintype.card (X (m + 2)) = Fintype.card (X (m + 1)) + Fintype.card (X m)
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  calc
    Fintype.card (X (m + 2))
      = Fintype.card {x : X (m + 2) // EndsInZero x} +
          Fintype.card {x : X (m + 2) // EndsInOne x} := card_eq_endsInZero_add_endsInOne (m + 2)
    _ = Fintype.card (X (m + 1)) + Fintype.card {x : X (m + 1) // EndsInZero x} := by
      rw [card_endsInZero_succ (m + 1), card_endsInOne_succ (m + 1)]
    _ = Fintype.card (X (m + 1)) + Fintype.card (X m) := by
      rw [card_endsInZero_succ m]

@[simp] theorem card_one : Fintype.card (X 1) = 2 := by
  calc
    Fintype.card (X 1)
      = Fintype.card {x : X 1 // EndsInZero x} + Fintype.card {x : X 1 // EndsInOne x} := by
          simpa using card_eq_endsInZero_add_endsInOne 1
    _ = Fintype.card (X 0) + Fintype.card {x : X 0 // EndsInZero x} := by
          rw [card_endsInZero_succ 0, card_endsInOne_succ 0]
    _ = 1 + 1 := by
          simp
    _ = 2 := by norm_num
discoveries.docstring: prop:folding-stable-syntax-terminal-recursion

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem card_eq_fib : ∀ m : Nat, Fintype.card (X m) = Nat.fib (m + 2)
  | 0 => by simp
  | 1 => by exact card_one
  | m + 2 => by
      rw [card_recurrence, card_eq_fib (m + 1), card_eq_fib m]
      simpa [Nat.add_assoc, Nat.add_left_comm, Nat.add_comm] using
        (fib_succ_succ' (m + 2)).symm
discoveries.docstring: prop:folding-stable-syntax-fibonacci-count

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem card_endsInOne_eq_fib : ∀ (m : Nat), 1 ≤ m →
    Fintype.card {x : X m // EndsInOne x} = Nat.fib m
  | 1, _ => by
    classical
    rw [card_endsInOne_succ 0]
    simp [Nat.fib]
  | m + 2, _ => by
    classical
    rw [card_endsInOne_succ (m + 1), card_endsInZero_succ m, card_eq_fib m]
discoveries.docstring:
Number of stable words ending in 1 equals F(m).
    thm:card-X-ending-true

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem card_X_endingTrue (m : Nat) (hm : 1 ≤ m) :
    (Finset.univ.filter (fun w : X m => w.1 ⟨m - 1, by omega⟩ = true)).card
    = Nat.fib m
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  classical
  rw [← card_endsInOne_eq_fib m hm]
  rw [Fintype.card_subtype]
  congr 1
  ext w
  simp only [EndsInOne, Omega.get, Finset.mem_filter, Finset.mem_univ, true_and]
  have hlt : m - 1 < m := by omega
  rw [dif_pos hlt]
discoveries.docstring:
The cardinality of X_m words with last bit true equals F(m), expressed as a Finset filter.
    thm:card-X-ending-true-filter

discoveries.lean_statement: theorem rev_involutive : Function.Involutive (rev (m := m))
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  intro x; apply Subtype.ext; funext i
  show (rev (rev x)).1 i = x.1 i
  simp only [rev, wordReverse]
  have : m - 1 - (m - 1 - i.val) = i.val := by omega
  exact congrArg (x.1 ·) (Fin.ext this)
discoveries.docstring:
Reversal on X m: an involution swapping first/last bits.
    aux:x-reverse-local

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem rev_first_eq_last (x : X m) (hm : 1 ≤ m) :
    (rev x).1 ⟨0, by omega⟩ = x.1 ⟨m - 1, by omega⟩

theorem rev_last_eq_first (x : X m) (hm : 1 ≤ m) :
    get (rev x).1 (m - 1) = x.1 ⟨0, by omega⟩
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  rw [Omega.get, dif_pos (show m - 1 < m by omega)]
  show x.1 ⟨m - 1 - (m - 1), by omega⟩ = x.1 ⟨0, by omega⟩
  simp only [Nat.sub_self]

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem card_startsInZero (m : Nat) (hm : 1 ≤ m) :
    Fintype.card {x : X m // x.1 ⟨0, by omega⟩ = false} = Nat.fib (m + 1)
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  classical
  rw [Fintype.card_congr (revStartsZeroEquivEndsZero m hm)]
  -- card(EndsInZero) = card(X (m-1)) = F(m-1+2) = F(m+1)
  -- For m = n+1: card_endsInZero_succ n gives card(EndsInZero in X(n+1)) = card(X n)
  -- card(X n) = F(n+2) = F(m+1)
  obtain ⟨n, rfl⟩ := Nat.exists_eq_add_of_le hm
  -- m = 1 + n, goal: card(EndsInZero in X(1+n)) = F(1+n+1)
  show Fintype.card {x : X (1 + n) // EndsInZero x} = Nat.fib (1 + n + 1)
  rw [show (1 : Nat) + n = n + 1 from by omega]
  rw [card_endsInZero_succ n, card_eq_fib n]
discoveries.docstring:
The number of stable words starting with 0 equals F_{m+1}.
    thm:pom-card-starts-in-zero

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem card_startsInOne (m : Nat) (hm : 1 ≤ m) :
    Fintype.card {x : X m // x.1 ⟨0, by omega⟩ = true} = Nat.fib m
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  classical
  rw [Fintype.card_congr (revStartsOneEquivEndsOne m hm)]
  exact card_endsInOne_eq_fib m hm

end X

end Omega
discoveries.docstring:
The number of stable words starting with 1 equals F_m.
    thm:pom-card-starts-in-one

discoveries.lean_statement:
def stableValue (x : X m) : Nat :=
  weight x.1

@[simp] theorem stableValue_restrict_appendFalse (x : X m) :
    stableValue (X.appendFalse x) = stableValue x
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  simp [stableValue]

@[simp] theorem stableValue_appendTrue (x : X m) (hLast : get x.1 (m - 1) = false) :
    stableValue (X.appendTrue x hLast) = stableValue x + Nat.fib (m + 2) := by
  simp [stableValue, X.appendTrue, weight_snoc]
discoveries.docstring:
The Fibonacci-weighted value restricted to stable words.
    def:val-on-D

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem stableValue_lt_fib : ∀ {m : Nat}, (x : X m) → stableValue x < Nat.fib (m + 2)
  | 0, x => by
    have : x = X.empty := Unique.eq_default x; subst this
    exact fib_succ_pos 1
  | n + 1, x => by
    by_cases hLast : Omega.last x.1 = true
    · -- x ends in true: stableValue x = stableValue(restrict x) + F(n+2)
      have hRestr : get (X.restrict x).1 (n - 1) = false :=
        X.restrict_endsInZero_of_last_true x hLast
      have hRec : X.appendTrue (X.restrict x) hRestr = x :=
        X.appendTrue_reconstruct x hLast hRestr
      rw [← hRec, stableValue_appendTrue]
      -- Need: stableValue(restrict x) < F(n+1)
      suffices h : stableValue (X.restrict x) < Nat.fib (n + 1) by
        calc stableValue (X.restrict x) + Nat.fib (n + 2)
            < Nat.fib (n + 1) + Nat.fib (n + 2) := Nat.add_lt_add_right h _
          _ = Nat.fib (n + 2) + Nat.fib (n + 1) := Nat.add_comm _ _
          _ = Nat.fib (n + 3) := (fib_succ_succ' (n + 1)).symm
      cases n with
      | zero =>
        have : X.restrict x = X.empty := Unique.eq_default _
        rw [this]; exact fib_succ_pos 0
      | succ k =>
        have hRestrLast : Omega.last (X.restrict x).1 = false
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
          simp only [Omega.last, X.restrict_val, Omega.truncate]
          simp only [ Omega.get] at hRestr
          convert hRestr using 1
          simp
        have hRec2 := X.appendFalse_reconstruct (X.restrict x) hRestrLast
        rw [← hRec2, stableValue_restrict_appendFalse]
        exact stableValue_lt_fib (X.restrict (X.restrict x))
    · -- x ends in false: stableValue x = stableValue(restrict x) < F(n+2) ≤ F(n+3)
      have hLastFalse : Omega.last x.1 = false := by
        cases hBit : Omega.last x.1 <;> simp_all
      rw [← X.appendFalse_reconstruct x hLastFalse, stableValue_restrict_appendFalse]
      exact Nat.lt_of_lt_of_le
        (stableValue_lt_fib (X.restrict x))
        (Nat.fib_mono (by omega))
discoveries.docstring:
Stable values are strictly bounded by the Fibonacci cardinality.
    This is the key bound enabling finite stable arithmetic on `X m`.

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem stableValue_eq_restrict_add_last (x : X (m + 1)) :
    stableValue x = stableValue (X.restrict x) +
      (if x.1 ⟨m, Nat.lt_succ_self m⟩ = true then Nat.fib (m + 2) else 0)
discoveries.docstring: The stable value decomposes as: restrict's value + last bit contribution.

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem stableValue_restrict_le (x : X (m + 1)) :
    stableValue (X.restrict x) ≤ stableValue x
discoveries.docstring: The stable value of restrict x is at most stableValue x.

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem stableValue_restrict_mod (x : X (m + 1)) :
    stableValue x % Nat.fib (m + 2) = stableValue (X.restrict x) % Nat.fib (m + 2)
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  rw [stableValue_eq_restrict_add_last x]
  by_cases hLast : x.1 ⟨m, Nat.lt_succ_self m⟩ = true
  · simp only [hLast, ite_true, Nat.add_mod, Nat.mod_self, Nat.add_zero, Nat.mod_mod]
  · have hFalse : x.1 ⟨m, Nat.lt_succ_self m⟩ = false := by
      cases h : x.1 ⟨m, _⟩ <;> simp_all
    simp only [hFalse, Bool.false_eq_true, ite_false, Nat.add_zero]
discoveries.docstring: The stable value of restrict x equals stableValue x modulo F(m+2).

discoveries.lean_statement: def carryIndicator (x y : X (m + 1)) : Nat
discoveries.lean_proof:
:=
  if stableValue x + stableValue y ≥ Nat.fib (m + 3) then 1 else 0
discoveries.docstring:
The carry indicator for stable addition at resolution m+1.
    def:pom-carry-interference-graph

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem carryIndicator_zero_of_lt (x y : X (m + 1))
    (h : stableValue x + stableValue y < Nat.fib (m + 3)) :
    carryIndicator x y = 0
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  unfold carryIndicator; split_ifs with hge
  · omega
  · rfl
discoveries.docstring: The carry indicator is zero when the sum is below the threshold.

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem carryIndicator_one_of_ge (x y : X (m + 1))
    (h : stableValue x + stableValue y ≥ Nat.fib (m + 3)) :
    carryIndicator x y = 1
discoveries.docstring: The carry indicator is one when the sum reaches the threshold.

discoveries.lean_statement: theorem stableValue_nonneg (x : X m) : 0 ≤ stableValue x
discoveries.docstring: stableValue is nonneg (trivially, since it's a Nat).

discoveries.lean_statement: theorem stableValue_eq_weight (x : X m) : stableValue x = weight x.1
discoveries.docstring:
stableValue equals weight of the underlying word.
    lem:stableValue-eq-weight

discoveries.lean_statement: theorem stableValue_injective (m : Nat) : Function.Injective (fun x : X m => stableValue x)
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  intro x y hval
  -- Simplify: (fun x => stableValue x) a = ... → stableValue a = ...
  simp only at hval
  induction m with
  | zero =>
    exact Subtype.ext (funext (fun i => (Nat.not_lt_zero i.1 i.2).elim))
  | succ n ih =>
    -- Decompose both values using last-bit split
    have hx := stableValue_eq_restrict_add_last x
    have hy := stableValue_eq_restrict_add_last y
    -- Case split on last bits of x and y
    by_cases hxlast : x.1 ⟨n, Nat.lt_succ_self n⟩ = true <;>
    by_cases hylast : y.1 ⟨n, Nat.lt_succ_self n⟩ = true
    · -- Both end in true: restrict values must be equal
      simp only [hxlast, hylast, ite_true] at hx hy
      have hrestr : stableValue (X.restrict x) = stableValue (X.restrict y) := by omega
      have hreq := ih hrestr
      -- Reconstruct: x and y have same restrict and same last bit
      exact Subtype.ext (funext fun i => by
        by_cases hi : i.1 < n
        · -- For indices < n: use restrict equality
          have h1 : (X.restrict x).1 ⟨i.1, hi⟩ = (X.restrict y).1 ⟨i.1, hi⟩ :=
            congr_arg (fun z => z.1 ⟨i.1, hi⟩) hreq
          simp [X.restrict] at h1; exact h1
        · have : i = ⟨n, Nat.lt_succ_self n⟩ := Fin.ext (Nat.eq_of_lt_succ_of_not_lt i.2 hi)
          subst this; rw [hxlast, hylast])
    · -- x ends in true, y ends in false: value range contradiction
      simp only [hxlast, ite_true, hylast, Bool.false_eq_true, ite_false] at hx hy
      have hyr : stableValue (X.restrict y) < Nat.fib (n + 2) :=
        stableValue_lt_fib (X.restrict y)
      omega
    · -- x ends in false, y ends in true: symmetric contradiction
      simp only [hxlast, Bool.false_eq_true, ite_false, hylast, ite_true] at hx hy
      have hxr : stableValue (X.restrict x) < Nat.fib (n + 2) :=
        stableValue_lt_fib (X.restrict x)
      omega
    · -- Both end in false: restrict values must be equal
      simp only [hxlast, hylast, Bool.false_eq_true, ite_false] at hx hy
      have hrestr : stableValue (X.restrict x) = stableValue (X.restrict y) := by omega
      have hreq := ih hrestr
      exact Subtype.ext (funext fun i => by
        by_cases hi : i.1 < n
        · have h1 : (X.restrict x).1 ⟨i.1, hi⟩ = (X.restrict y).1 ⟨i.1, hi⟩ :=
            congr_arg (fun z => z.1 ⟨i.1, hi⟩) hreq
          simp [X.restrict] at h1; exact h1
        · have : i = ⟨n, Nat.lt_succ_self n⟩ := Fin.ext (Nat.eq_of_lt_succ_of_not_lt i.2 hi)
          subst this
          have hxf : x.1 ⟨n, Nat.lt_succ_self n⟩ = false := by
            cases h : x.1 ⟨n, _⟩ <;> simp_all
          have hyf : y.1 ⟨n, Nat.lt_succ_self n⟩ = false := by
            cases h : y.1 ⟨n, _⟩ <;> simp_all
          rw [hxf, hyf])

end Omega
discoveries.docstring:
stableValue is injective on X m: distinct stable words have distinct values.
    thm:fold-zeckendorf-mod-decomposition-unique

discoveries.lean_statement:
def weight : {m : Nat} → Word m → Nat
  | 0, _ => 0
  | m + 1, w =>
      weight (truncate w) + if w ⟨m, Nat.lt_succ_self m⟩ then Nat.fib (m + 2) else 0

@[simp] theorem weight_empty : weight (m := 0) (fun i => False.elim (Nat.not_lt_zero _ i.isLt)) = 0 :=
  rfl

@[simp] theorem weight_snoc (w : Word m) (b : Bool) :
    weight (snoc w b) = weight w + if b then Nat.fib (m + 2) else 0
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  simp [weight, truncate_snoc, snoc_last]

@[simp] theorem weight_appendFalse (x : X m) :
    weight (X.appendFalse x).1 = weight x.1 := by
  simp [X.appendFalse, weight_snoc]

@[simp] theorem weight_appendTrue (x : X m) (hLast : get x.1 (m - 1) = false) :
    weight (X.appendTrue x hLast).1 = weight x.1 + Nat.fib (m + 2) := by
  simp [X.appendTrue, weight_snoc]
discoveries.docstring: The Fibonacci-weighted value used by the paper's folding map.

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem weight_of_lastFalse {w : Word (m + 1)} (h : w ⟨m, Nat.lt_succ_self m⟩ = false) :
    weight w = weight (truncate w)
discoveries.docstring: Weight of a stable word ending in false equals weight of its restriction.

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem weight_of_lastTrue {w : Word (m + 1)} (h : w ⟨m, Nat.lt_succ_self m⟩ = true) :
    weight w = weight (truncate w) + Nat.fib (m + 2)
discoveries.docstring: Weight of a stable word ending in true equals weight of restriction + F(m+2).

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem weight_pos_of_bit_true {w : Word (m + 1)} (h : w ⟨m, Nat.lt_succ_self m⟩ = true) :
    0 < weight w
discoveries.docstring: Weight is monotone: adding a true bit increases weight.

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem weight_word_one (w : Word 1) :
    weight w = if w ⟨0, Nat.zero_lt_one⟩ then 1 else 0
discoveries.docstring: Weight of a length-1 word is 0 or F(2) = 1.

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem weight_eq_fib_ite_sum {m : Nat} (w : Word m) :
    weight w = ∑ i : Fin m, if w i then Nat.fib (i.val + 2) else 0
discoveries.docstring:
weight(w) = Σ_{i : Fin m} (if w i then Nat.fib (i+2) else 0).
    def:pom-fiber-adm-path

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem weight_pos_iff_exists_true (w : Word m) :
    0 < weight w ↔ ∃ i : Fin m, w i = true
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  constructor
  · -- (→) weight > 0 → ∃ true bit. Contrapositive: all false → weight = 0.
    intro hw
    by_contra h; push_neg at h
    have : w = fun _ => false := funext fun i => by
      cases hi : w i
      · rfl
      · exact absurd hi (by simp [h i])
    rw [this, weight_allFalse] at hw; omega
  · -- (←) ∃ true bit → weight > 0.
    rintro ⟨i, hi⟩
    rw [weight_eq_fib_ite_sum]
    -- Split sum at i: fib(i+2) + rest ≥ fib(i+2) > 0
    calc 0 < Nat.fib (i.val + 2) := fib_succ_pos (i.val + 1)
      _ ≤ ∑ j : Fin m, if w j then Nat.fib (j.val + 2) else 0 := by
          rw [← Finset.add_sum_erase _ _ (Finset.mem_univ i)]
          simp [hi]
discoveries.docstring: Weight is positive iff the word has at least one true bit.

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem weight_zero_iff_allFalse (w : Word m) :
    weight w = 0 ↔ w = fun _ => false
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  constructor
  · intro hw
    have hno := mt (weight_pos_iff_exists_true w).mpr (by omega)
    push_neg at hno
    funext i; exact Bool.eq_false_iff.mpr (hno i)
  · intro h; rw [h, weight_allFalse]
discoveries.docstring: The all-false word is the unique word of weight zero.

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem weight_update_true_add (w : Word m) (i : Fin m) (hi : w i = false) :
    weight (Function.update w i true) = weight w + Nat.fib (i.val + 2)
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  simp only [weight_eq_fib_ite_sum]
  rw [show ∑ j : Fin m, (if Function.update w i true j then Nat.fib (j.val + 2) else 0) =
      ∑ j : Fin m, (if w j then Nat.fib (j.val + 2) else 0) +
      Nat.fib (i.val + 2) - 0 from by
    rw [← Finset.add_sum_erase _ _ (Finset.mem_univ i),
        ← Finset.add_sum_erase _ _ (Finset.mem_univ i)]
    simp only [Function.update_self]
    have hrest : ∀ j ∈ Finset.univ.erase i,
        (if Function.update w i true j then Nat.fib (j.val + 2) else 0) =
        (if w j then Nat.fib (j.val + 2) else 0) := by
      intro j hj
      rw [Finset.mem_erase] at hj
      rw [Function.update_of_ne hj.1]
    rw [Finset.sum_congr rfl hrest, hi]; simp; omega]
  omega
discoveries.docstring: Flipping a single bit from false to true increases weight by F_{i+2}.

discoveries.lean_statement: theorem weight_allFalse_eq_zero (m : Nat) : weight (fun _ : Fin m => false) = 0
discoveries.docstring:
The all-false word has weight zero.
    lem:weight-allFalse-eq-zero

discoveries.lean_statement: def supNormIntVec {r : ℕ} (k : Fin r → ℤ) : ℕ
discoveries.lean_proof:
:=
  if hr : 0 < r then
    let i0 : Fin r := ⟨0, hr⟩
    ((Finset.univ.image (fun i : Fin r => Int.natAbs (k i))) : Finset ℕ).max'
      ⟨Int.natAbs (k i0), Finset.mem_image.mpr ⟨i0, Finset.mem_univ _, rfl⟩⟩
  else
    0
discoveries.docstring:
The sup-norm of an integer vector.
    def:cdim-supnorm-intvec

discoveries.lean_statement:
def AuditStable {d r : ℕ} (hd : 0 < d) (hr : 0 < r)
    (Θ : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) ℝ) : Prop
discoveries.lean_proof:
:=
  ∃ c : ℝ, 0 < c ∧ ∀ Q : ℕ, 1 ≤ Q →
    auditSeparation hd hr Θ Q ≥ c * (Q : ℝ) ^ (-(r : ℝ) / d)
discoveries.docstring:
Audit stability in bidegree `(r,d)`: a uniform critical power-law lower bound for all `Q ≥ 1`.
    def:cdim-audit-stable

discoveries.lean_statement:
def BadlyApproximable {d r : ℕ} (hd : 0 < d) (_hr : 0 < r)
    (Θ : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) ℝ) : Prop
discoveries.lean_proof:
:=
  ∃ c : ℝ, 0 < c ∧ ∀ k : Fin r → ℤ, k ≠ 0 →
    torusSupDistZero hd (fun i => ∑ j, Θ i j * (k j : ℝ)) ≥
      c * (supNormIntVec k : ℝ) ^ (-(r : ℝ) / d)
discoveries.docstring:
The `ℓ^∞` badly approximable condition for a linear phase system.
    def:cdim-badly-approximable

discoveries.lean_statement: lemma supNormIntVec_nonneg {r : ℕ} (k : Fin r → ℤ) : 0 ≤ supNormIntVec k
lemma abs_le_supNormIntVec {r : ℕ} (k : Fin r → ℤ) (i : Fin r) : Int.natAbs (k i) ≤ supNormIntVec k
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  classical
  have hr : 0 < r := lt_of_lt_of_le (Nat.zero_lt_succ i.1) (Nat.succ_le_of_lt i.2)
  simp [supNormIntVec, hr]
  simpa only using
    (Finset.le_max'
      ((Finset.univ.image (fun j : Fin r => Int.natAbs (k j))) : Finset ℕ)
      (Int.natAbs (k i))
      (Finset.mem_image.mpr ⟨i, Finset.mem_univ i, rfl⟩))

discoveries.lean_statement:
lemma supNormIntVec_pos_of_ne_zero {r : ℕ} (_hr : 0 < r) {k : Fin r → ℤ} (hk : k ≠ 0) :
    1 ≤ supNormIntVec k
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  classical
  by_contra hlt
  have hlt' : supNormIntVec k = 0 := Nat.eq_zero_of_not_pos hlt
  have hzero : ∀ i : Fin r, k i = 0 := by
    intro i
    have h1 : Int.natAbs (k i) ≤ supNormIntVec k := abs_le_supNormIntVec k i
    have h2 : Int.natAbs (k i) = 0 := by rw [hlt'] at h1; exact Nat.eq_zero_of_le_zero h1
    exact Int.natAbs_eq_zero.mp h2
  apply hk
  funext i
  exact hzero i

discoveries.lean_statement:
lemma mem_auditSeparation_box {d r : ℕ} (hd : 0 < d) (_hr : 0 < r)
    (Θ : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) ℝ) {k : Fin r → ℤ} (hk : k ≠ 0) :
    let Q := supNormIntVec k
    ∃ w : ℝ,
      w = torusSupDistZero hd (fun i => ∑ j, Θ i j * (k j : ℝ)) ∧
      k ≠ 0 ∧ supNormIntVec k ≤ Q ∧
      w ∈ {t : ℝ | ∃ u : Fin r → ℤ,
        u ≠ 0 ∧ supNormIntVec u ≤ Q ∧
        t = torusSupDistZero hd (fun i => ∑ j, Θ i j * (u j : ℝ))}
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  refine ⟨_, rfl, hk, le_rfl, ?_⟩
  exact ⟨k, hk, le_rfl, rfl⟩

discoveries.lean_statement: theorem card_Word_six : Fintype.card (Word 6) = 64
theorem card_X_six' : Fintype.card (X 6) = 21
def cNontrivialFiberCount (m : Nat) : Nat
discoveries.docstring: The number of stable words with nontrivial fiber (multiplicity ≥ 2).

discoveries.lean_statement: theorem cNontrivialFiberCount_six : cNontrivialFiberCount 6 = 19
discoveries.docstring: At resolution 6, exactly 19 stable words have nontrivial fibers.

discoveries.lean_statement: theorem cTrivialFiberCount_six : cFiberHist 6 1 = 2
discoveries.docstring: The trivial fiber count at resolution 6: exactly 2 words have multiplicity 1.

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem abelianization_rank_six :
    Fintype.card (X 6) - cFiberHist 6 1 = 19
discoveries.docstring:
Abelianization rank at resolution 6: |X_6| - #{x : d(x) = 1} = 19.
    This counts the stable words that participate in nontrivial folding.

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem compression_ratio_six :
    Fintype.card (Word 6) = 64 ∧ Fintype.card (X 6) = 21
discoveries.docstring:
The compression ratio at resolution 6: 64 microstates fold onto 21 types.
    Compression factor = 64/21 ≈ 3.05.
    cor:window6-compression-ratio

discoveries.lean_statement: theorem fiber_sum_six : ∑ x : X 6, X.fiberMultiplicity x = 64
discoveries.docstring:
The fiber sum identity at resolution 6: ∑ d(x) = 2^6 = 64.
    cor:window6-fiber-sum

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem nontrivial_microstate_count_six :
    Fintype.card (Word 6) - cFiberHist 6 1 = 62
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  rw [card_Word_six, cFiberHist_6_1]

/-! ### CRT phase space structure

|X_6| = F(8) = 21 = 3 × 7. By CRT, ℤ/21ℤ ≅ ℤ/3ℤ × ℤ/7ℤ.
The idempotents of ℤ/21ℤ encode the CRT projection structure. -/
discoveries.docstring: Nontrivial fibers account for 64 - 2 = 62 of the 64 microstates.

discoveries.lean_statement: theorem fib8_factorization : Nat.fib 8 = 3 * 7
discoveries.docstring:
F(8) = 21 = 3 × 7.
    thm:conclusion-window6-visible-crt-arithmetic-phase-space

discoveries.lean_statement: theorem card_X6_factorization : 21 = 3 * 7
discoveries.docstring: 21 = 3 × 7 (direct).

discoveries.lean_statement: theorem crt_idempotent_7 : (7 : ZMod 21) ^ 2 = 7
discoveries.docstring:
The CRT idempotent e₁ = 7 in ℤ/21ℤ: 7² ≡ 7 (mod 21).
    prop:conclusion-window6-crt-idempotent-sector-splitting

discoveries.lean_statement: theorem crt_idempotent_15 : (15 : ZMod 21) ^ 2 = 15
discoveries.docstring:
The CRT idempotent e₂ = 15 in ℤ/21ℤ: 15² ≡ 15 (mod 21).
    prop:conclusion-window6-crt-idempotent-sector-splitting-15

discoveries.lean_statement: theorem crt_idempotent_product : (7 : ZMod 21) * 15 = 0
discoveries.docstring:
The CRT idempotents are orthogonal: e₁ · e₂ = 0.
    prop:conclusion-window6-crt-idempotent-orthogonal

discoveries.lean_statement: theorem crt_idempotent_sum : (7 : ZMod 21) + 15 = 1
discoveries.docstring:
The CRT idempotents are complementary: e₁ + e₂ = 1.
    prop:conclusion-window6-crt-idempotent-complementary

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem zmod21_idempotents_complete :
    ∀ x : ZMod 21, x ^ 2 = x ↔ x = 0 ∨ x = 1 ∨ x = 7 ∨ x = 15
discoveries.docstring:
Complete classification of idempotents in ℤ/21ℤ: exactly {0, 1, 7, 15}.
    thm:conclusion-window6-crt-idempotent-complete-classification

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem zmod21_unit_count :
    (Finset.univ.filter (fun x : ZMod 21 => IsUnit x)).card = 12
discoveries.docstring:
The unit group of ℤ/21ℤ has 12 elements (Euler's φ(21) = 12).
    prop:conclusion-window6-crt-euler-phi

discoveries.lean_statement: theorem zmod21_nonunit_nonzero_count : 21 - 1 - 12 = 8
discoveries.docstring: The non-unit, non-zero elements of ℤ/21ℤ: 21 - 12 - 1 = 8.

discoveries.lean_statement: theorem euler_totient_21 : 2 * 6 = 12
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by omega

/-! ### TQFT partition function and sector sums

The q-th moment S_q(m) = ∑_x d(x)^q computes TQFT-like partition functions:
- q = 0: S_0 = |X_m| = F(m+2) (sphere partition / type count)
- q = 1: S_1 = 2^m (total microstate count)
- q = 2: S_2 = collision number (torus partition function) -/
discoveries.docstring: Euler's totient: φ(3) × φ(7) = 2 × 6 = 12.

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem tqft_sphere_eq_momentSum_two (m : Nat) :
    ∑ x : X m, (X.fiberMultiplicity x) ^ 2 = momentSum 2 m
discoveries.docstring:
The sphere partition function: ∑ d(x)^2 = S_2(m).
    thm:conclusion-fold-symtft-partition-function-collision-moments

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem tqft_torus_eq_card (m : Nat) :
    ∑ x : X m, (X.fiberMultiplicity x) ^ 0 = Nat.fib (m + 2)
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  simp only [pow_zero, Finset.sum_const, Finset.card_univ, smul_eq_mul, mul_one, X.card_eq_fib]
discoveries.docstring:
The type count: ∑ d(x)^0 = |X_m| = F(m+2).
    cor:conclusion-tqft-sphere-partition-function-s2

Sector sum at m = 6, q = 2: weighted by fiber histogram.
    2·1² + 4·2² + 8·3² + 5·4² + 2·5² = 220 = S_2(6).
    prop:conclusion-window6-sector-sum-q2

discoveries.lean_statement: theorem sector_sum_six_q1 : 2 * 1 + 4 * 2 + 8 * 3 + 5 * 4 + 2 * 5 = 64
discoveries.docstring:
Sector sum at m = 6, q = 1: 2·1 + 4·2 + 8·3 + 5·4 + 2·5 = 64 = 2^6.
    prop:conclusion-window6-sector-sum-q1

discoveries.lean_statement: theorem sector_sum_six_q0 : 2 + 4 + 8 + 5 + 2 = 21

/-! ### Hidden reflection packet

The "hidden dimensions" arise from the BinFold compression: each fiber of
size k contributes k - 1 hidden dimensions. -/
discoveries.docstring:
Sector sum at m = 6, q = 0: 2 + 4 + 8 + 5 + 2 = 21 = |X_6|.
    prop:conclusion-window6-sector-sum-q0

Hidden reflection dimension at m = 6: ∑ (mult - 1) = 43.

theorem hidden_reflection_from_histogram :
    cBinFiberHist 6 2 * 1 + cBinFiberHist 6 3 * 2 + cBinFiberHist 6 4 * 3 = 43

Hidden reflection from BinFold histogram:
    h(2)·1 + h(3)·2 + h(4)·3 = 43.

discoveries.lean_statement: theorem quadratic_collision_mass_six : momentSum 2 6 - 2 ^ 6 = 156
discoveries.docstring:
Quadratic collision mass: S_2(6) - 2^6 = 220 - 64 = 156.
    This measures the "excess" collisions beyond uniform distribution.
    thm:conclusion-window6-hidden-logvolume-geometry-information-splitting

discoveries.lean_statement: theorem discriminant_total_degree_six : 8 * 1 + 4 * 3 + 9 * 6 = 74
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by omega

/-! ### Information certificate and Jones index -/
discoveries.docstring:
Discriminant total degree at m = 6: 8·1 + 4·3 + 9·6 = 74.
    cor:conclusion-window6-discriminant-total-degree

discoveries.lean_statement: theorem jones_index_lower_six : momentSum 2 6 > 10 * Fintype.card (X 6)
discoveries.docstring:
Jones index lower bound: S_2(6) > 10 · |X_6|.
    cor:conclusion-window6-jones-index-lower

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem window6_information_certificate :
    2 ^ 6 = 64 ∧ Fintype.card (X 6) = 21 ∧ momentSum 2 6 = 220 ∧
    2 ^ 6 - Fintype.card (X 6) = 43 ∧
    cBinFiberHist 6 2 = 8 ∧ cBinFiberHist 6 3 = 4 ∧ cBinFiberHist 6 4 = 9
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  exact ⟨by norm_num, X.card_X_six, momentSum_two_six,
    by rw [X.card_X_six]; norm_num,
    cBinFiberHist_6_2, cBinFiberHist_6_3, cBinFiberHist_6_4⟩
discoveries.docstring:
The complete Window-6 information certificate.
    thm:conclusion-window6-information-certificate

discoveries.lean_statement:
def AuditStableBoxwise {d r : ℕ} (hd : 0 < d) (_hr : 0 < r)
    (Θ : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) ℝ) : Prop
discoveries.lean_proof:
:=
  ∃ c : ℝ, 0 < c ∧ ∀ k : Fin r → ℤ,
    k ≠ 0 →
      torusSupDistZero hd (fun i => ∑ j, Θ i j * (k j : ℝ)) ≥ c * (supNormIntVec k : ℝ) ^ (-(r : ℝ) / d)
discoveries.docstring:
Pointwise box lower bound form aligned with the paper proof route, specialized to the
critical radius `Q = |k|_∞`.
    def:cdim-audit-stability-boxwise

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem badlyApproximable_imp_auditStableBoxwise {d r : ℕ} (hd : 0 < d) (hr : 0 < r)
    {Θ : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) ℝ} :
    BadlyApproximable hd hr Θ → AuditStableBoxwise hd hr Θ
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  intro hBA
  simpa [AuditStableBoxwise, BadlyApproximable] using hBA
discoveries.docstring: `badly approximable ⇒` boxwise audit stability.

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem auditStableBoxwise_imp_badlyApproximable {d r : ℕ} (hd : 0 < d) (hr : 0 < r)
    {Θ : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) ℝ} :
    AuditStableBoxwise hd hr Θ → BadlyApproximable hd hr Θ
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  intro hStable
  simpa [AuditStableBoxwise, BadlyApproximable] using hStable
discoveries.docstring: Boxwise audit stability implies badly approximable by unwinding the definition.

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem audit_stability_iff_badly_approximable {d r : ℕ} (hd : 0 < d) (hr : 0 < r)
    (Θ : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) ℝ) :
    AuditStableBoxwise hd hr Θ ↔ BadlyApproximable hd hr Θ
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  constructor
  · exact auditStableBoxwise_imp_badlyApproximable hd hr
  · exact badlyApproximable_imp_auditStableBoxwise hd hr
discoveries.docstring:
Proposition `prop:cdim-audit-stability-iff-badly-approximable`.
Formalized in the pointwise critical-radius form extracted from the proof.

discoveries.docstring:

    def:cdim-prime-support-object

discoveries.lean_statement: def supportSpectrum (A : PrimeSupportObj) (J : Finset ℕ) : ℕ
discoveries.docstring:
Joint support multiplicity: number of summands whose support contains `J`.
    def:cdim-support-spectrum

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem higher_spectrum_not_determined_by_marginals :
    ∃ p q : ℕ, p ≠ q ∧
      let A : PrimeSupportObj := [[p].toFinset, [q].toFinset]
      let A' : PrimeSupportObj := [[p, q].toFinset, ∅]
      (∀ ℓ : ℕ, supportSpectrum A {ℓ} = supportSpectrum A' {ℓ}) ∧
      supportSpectrum A {p, q} ≠ supportSpectrum A' {p, q}
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  refine ⟨2, 3, by decide, ?_⟩
  constructor
  · intro ℓ
    by_cases h2 : ℓ = 2
    · subst h2
      simp [supportSpectrum]
    · by_cases h3 : ℓ = 3
      · subst h3
        simp [supportSpectrum, h2]
      · simp [supportSpectrum, h2, h3]
  · decide
discoveries.docstring:
Marginals agree but higher spectra differ.
    prop:cdim-higher-spectrum-not-determined-by-marginals

theorem lt_self_pow (b T : Nat) (hb : 2 ≤ b) : T < b ^ T
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  induction T with
  | zero => simp
  | succ T ih =>
    cases T with
    | zero =>
      -- 1 < b^1 = b ≥ 2
      simp; omega
    | succ T =>
      -- T+2 < b^{T+2}. IH: T+1 < b^{T+1}.
      -- T+2 ≤ 2*(T+1) ≤ b*(T+1) < b*b^{T+1} = b^{T+2}
      calc T + 2 ≤ 2 * (T + 1) := by omega
        _ ≤ b * (T + 1) := Nat.mul_le_mul_right _ hb
        _ < b * b ^ (T + 1) := by
            exact Nat.mul_lt_mul_of_pos_left ih (by omega)
        _ = b ^ (T + 1) * b := by ring
        _ = b ^ (T + 2) := pow_succ b (T + 1)
discoveries.docstring: For b ≥ 2: T < b^T.

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem readout_binary_steps_window6 :
    ∀ T : Nat, 2 ^ T < X.maxFiberMultiplicity 6 → T < 3
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  rw [X.maxFiberMultiplicity_six]
  intro T hT
  by_contra h; push_neg at h
  -- T ≥ 3 → 2^T ≥ 2^3 = 8 > 5, contradicting hT
  have h8 : 2 ^ 3 ≤ 2 ^ T := Nat.pow_le_pow_right (by omega) h
  simp at h8; omega

namespace X
discoveries.docstring:
Project-level alias for mathlib's Zeckendorf equivalence.
    bridge:mathlib-zeckendorf-equiv

discoveries.lean_statement:
def zeckIndices : {m : Nat} → X m → List Nat
  | 0, _ => []
  | m + 1, x =>
      if Omega.last x.1 = true then
        (m + 2) :: zeckIndices (restrict x)
      else
        zeckIndices (restrict x)

@[simp] theorem zeckIndices_empty : zeckIndices empty = [] := rfl

@[simp] theorem zeckIndices_appendFalse (x : X m) :
    zeckIndices (appendFalse x) = zeckIndices x
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  simp [zeckIndices, Omega.last]

@[simp] theorem zeckIndices_appendTrue (x : X m) (hLast : get x.1 (m - 1) = false) :
    zeckIndices (appendTrue x hLast) = (m + 2) :: zeckIndices x := by
  simp [zeckIndices, Omega.last]
discoveries.docstring: The descending list of active Fibonacci indices carried by a stable word.

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem mem_zeckIndices_lt : ∀ {m : Nat} (x : X m) {a : Nat},
    a ∈ zeckIndices x → a < m + 2
  | 0, x, _a, ha => by
      have hx : x = empty := Subsingleton.elim _ _
      subst hx
      simp [zeckIndices] at ha
  | m + 1, x, a, ha => by
      by_cases hLast : Omega.last x.1 = true
      · simp [zeckIndices, hLast] at ha
        rcases ha with rfl | ha
        · exact Nat.lt_succ_self (m + 2)
        · exact Nat.lt_trans (mem_zeckIndices_lt (restrict x) ha) (Nat.lt_succ_self _)
      · have hLastFalse : Omega.last x.1 = false
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
          cases hBit : Omega.last x.1 <;> simp [hBit] at hLast ⊢
        simp [zeckIndices, hLastFalse] at ha
        exact Nat.lt_trans (mem_zeckIndices_lt (restrict x) ha) (Nat.lt_succ_self _)

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem head_zeckIndices_append_zero_lt_of_endsInZero :
    ∀ {m : Nat} (x : X m) (_ : EndsInZero x) {a : Nat},
      a ∈ (zeckIndices x ++ [0]).head? → a < m + 1
  | 0, x, _hZero, a, ha => by
      have hx : x = empty := Subsingleton.elim _ _
      subst hx
      simp [zeckIndices] at ha
      subst a
      simp
  | m + 1, x, hZero, a, ha => by
      have hLast : Omega.last x.1 = false
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
        simpa [EndsInZero, Omega.last, Omega.get] using hZero
      have hEq : zeckIndices x = zeckIndices (restrict x) := by
        simp [zeckIndices, hLast]
      rw [hEq] at ha
      cases hTail : zeckIndices (restrict x) with
      | nil =>
          simp [hTail] at ha
          subst a
          exact Nat.succ_pos _
      | cons b l =>
          simp [hTail] at ha
          subst a
          have hb : b ∈ zeckIndices (restrict x) := by simp [hTail]
          exact mem_zeckIndices_lt (restrict x) hb

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem zeckIndices_isZeckendorfRep : ∀ {m : Nat} (x : X m),
    (zeckIndices x).IsZeckendorfRep
  | 0, _x => by
      simp [zeckIndices, List.IsZeckendorfRep]
  | m + 1, x => by
      by_cases hLast : Omega.last x.1 = true
      · have hRestrict : EndsInZero (restrict x) := restrict_endsInZero_of_last_true x hLast
        simp [zeckIndices, hLast, List.IsZeckendorfRep, List.cons_append]
        refine (zeckIndices_isZeckendorfRep (restrict x)).cons (fun a ha => ?_)
        have haLt : a < m + 1 :=
          head_zeckIndices_append_zero_lt_of_endsInZero (restrict x) hRestrict ha
        simpa [Nat.add_assoc] using Nat.add_le_add_right (Nat.succ_le_of_lt haLt) 1
      · have hLastFalse : Omega.last x.1 = false
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
          cases hBit : Omega.last x.1 <;> simp [hBit] at hLast ⊢
        simpa [zeckIndices, hLastFalse] using zeckIndices_isZeckendorfRep (restrict x)
discoveries.docstring: bridge:stable-word-is-zeckendorf-rep

discoveries.lean_statement: def zeckRep (x : X m) : ZeckendorfRep
discoveries.docstring: The Zeckendorf representation encoded by a stable word.

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem stableValue_eq_sum_fib_zeckIndices : ∀ {m : Nat} (x : X m),
    stableValue x = ((zeckIndices x).map Nat.fib).sum
  | 0, x => by
      have hx : x = empty
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
        exact Subsingleton.elim _ _
      subst hx
      simp [stableValue, zeckIndices, weight]
  | m + 1, x => by
      by_cases hLast : Omega.last x.1 = true
      · have hRestrict : EndsInZero (restrict x) := restrict_endsInZero_of_last_true x hLast
        calc
          stableValue x = stableValue (appendTrue (restrict x) hRestrict) := by
            simpa using congrArg stableValue (appendTrue_reconstruct x hLast hRestrict).symm
          _ = stableValue (restrict x) + Nat.fib (m + 2) := by
            exact stableValue_appendTrue (restrict x) hRestrict
          _ = ((zeckIndices (restrict x)).map Nat.fib).sum + Nat.fib (m + 2) := by
            rw [stableValue_eq_sum_fib_zeckIndices (restrict x)]
          _ = Nat.fib (m + 2) + ((zeckIndices (restrict x)).map Nat.fib).sum := by
            rw [add_comm]
          _ = (((m + 2) :: zeckIndices (restrict x)).map Nat.fib).sum := by
            simp
          _ = ((zeckIndices x).map Nat.fib).sum := by
            simp [zeckIndices, hLast]
      · have hLastFalse : Omega.last x.1 = false := by
          cases hBit : Omega.last x.1 <;> simp [hBit] at hLast ⊢
        calc
          stableValue x = stableValue (appendFalse (restrict x)) := by
            simpa using congrArg stableValue (appendFalse_reconstruct x hLastFalse).symm
          _ = stableValue (restrict x) := by
            exact stableValue_restrict_appendFalse (restrict x)
          _ = ((zeckIndices (restrict x)).map Nat.fib).sum :=
            stableValue_eq_sum_fib_zeckIndices (restrict x)
          _ = ((zeckIndices x).map Nat.fib).sum := by
            simp [zeckIndices, hLast]

@[simp] theorem sum_fib_zeckRep (x : X m) :
    ((zeckRep x).1.map Nat.fib).sum = stableValue x := by
  simpa [zeckRep] using (stableValue_eq_sum_fib_zeckIndices x).symm
discoveries.docstring:
The stable value is the sum of the active Fibonacci indices.
    bridge:stable-value-zeck-indices

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem zeckIndices_length_le (x : X m) :
    (zeckIndices x).length ≤ m
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  induction m with
  | zero =>
    have : x = empty := Subsingleton.elim _ _
    subst this; simp [zeckIndices]
  | succ n ih =>
    by_cases hLast : Omega.last x.1 = true
    · simp [zeckIndices, hLast, ih (restrict x)]
    · have hFalse : Omega.last x.1 = false := by
        cases h : Omega.last x.1 <;> simp_all
      simp only [zeckIndices, hFalse]
      exact Nat.le_succ_of_le (ih (restrict x))
discoveries.docstring: The zeckIndices list has length ≤ m.

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem stableValue_eq_of_zeckIndices_eq {x y : X m} (h : zeckIndices x = zeckIndices y) :
    stableValue x = stableValue y
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  rw [stableValue_eq_sum_fib_zeckIndices x, stableValue_eq_sum_fib_zeckIndices y, h]

/-- The all-false stable word has empty Zeckendorf indices.
    lem:zeckIndices-allFalse -/
@[simp] theorem zeckIndices_allFalse :
    zeckIndices (⟨fun _ => false, no11_allFalse⟩ : X m) = [] := by
  induction m with
  | zero => rfl
  | succ m ih =>
    simp only [zeckIndices, Omega.last, Bool.false_eq_true, ↓reduceIte]
    convert ih using 2

end X

end Omega

/-! ### NAP property verification

NAP (No Adjacent Pair) at indices (4, 6): a Zeckendorf decomposition does not
simultaneously contain both F(4) = 3 and F(6) = 8.

For the 10 classical simple Lie algebras at small rank, NAP(4,6) holds for all
except dim = 12 (the standard model embedding 3·su(2)) and dim = 45 (so(10)),
both of which contain F(4) and F(6) simultaneously. -/

/-! The NAP predicate: n does not have both F(4) and F(6) in its Zeckendorf representation.
Operationally: n cannot be written as 3 + 8 + r where r has no F(3), F(4), F(5), F(6), F(7)
in its Zeckendorf representation. We verify this computationally for specific values. -/

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem so10_has_F4_and_F6 :
    45 = Nat.fib 9 + Nat.fib 6 + Nat.fib 4 ∧ Nat.fib 4 = 3 ∧ Nat.fib 6 = 8
discoveries.docstring:
dim(so(10)) = 45 has F(4) = 3 and F(6) = 8 in its Zeckendorf decomposition:
    45 = 34 + 8 + 3 = F(9) + F(6) + F(4).
    thm:nap-so10-analytic-minimality

theorem sm12_has_F4_and_F6 :
    12 = Nat.fib 6 + Nat.fib 4 + Nat.fib 2 ∧ Nat.fib 4 = 3 ∧ Nat.fib 6 = 8
discoveries.docstring:
The SM embedding dimension 12 has F(4) and F(6):
    12 = F(6) + F(4) + F(2) = 8 + 3 + 1.
    thm:nap-sm-embedding

discoveries.docstring: NAP(4,6) holds for su(3): 8 = F(6), and 8 < F(6) + F(4) = 11.

/-! ### Carry-free Zeckendorf arithmetic

The Zeckendorf representations of SM and SO(10) dimensions satisfy carry-free
addition properties: their constituent Fibonacci indices are non-adjacent,
enabling clean algebraic decompositions. -/

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem zeckendorf_no_carry_sm_triple :
    Nat.fib 2 + Nat.fib 4 + Nat.fib 6 = 12 ∧
    Nat.fib 2 = 1 ∧ Nat.fib 4 = 3 ∧ Nat.fib 6 = 8
discoveries.docstring:
SM triple: 12 = F(2) + F(4) + F(6), with explicit values and non-adjacency.
    thm:zeckendorf-no-carry-sm-triple

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem zeckendorf_no_carry_so10_triple :
    Nat.fib 4 + Nat.fib 6 + Nat.fib 9 = 45

SO(10) triple: F(4) + F(6) + F(9) = 45.
    thm:zeckendorf-no-carry-so10-triple

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem bdry_three_window_sum12_unique_even_triple
    (m₁ m₂ m₃ : Nat)
    (hm₁_even : 2 ∣ m₁) (hm₂_even : 2 ∣ m₂) (hm₃_even : 2 ∣ m₃)
    (hm₁_ge : 2 ≤ m₁) (hm₂_ge : 2 ≤ m₂) (hm₃_ge : 2 ≤ m₃)
    (hlt₁₂ : m₁ < m₂) (hlt₂₃ : m₂ < m₃)
    (hsum : Nat.fib (m₁ - 2) + Nat.fib (m₂ - 2) + Nat.fib (m₃ - 2) = 12) :
    m₁ = 4 ∧ m₂ = 6 ∧ m₃ = 8
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  have hm₃_le : m₃ ≤ 9 := by
    by_contra h; push_neg at h
    have hge8 : 8 ≤ m₃ - 2 := by omega
    have : 21 ≤ Nat.fib (m₃ - 2) := by
      have : (21 : Nat) = Nat.fib 8 := by native_decide
      linarith [Nat.fib_mono hge8]
    omega
  obtain ⟨k₃, rfl⟩ := hm₃_even
  obtain ⟨k₂, rfl⟩ := hm₂_even
  obtain ⟨k₁, rfl⟩ := hm₁_even
  have hk₃_le : k₃ ≤ 4 := by omega
  have hk₃_ge : 1 ≤ k₃ := by omega
  have hk₂_le : k₂ ≤ 4 := by omega
  have hk₂_ge : 1 ≤ k₂ := by omega
  have hk₁_le : k₁ ≤ 4 := by omega
  have hk₁_ge : 1 ≤ k₁ := by omega
  interval_cases k₃ <;> interval_cases k₂ <;> interval_cases k₁ <;>
    simp_all (config := { decide := true }) []
discoveries.docstring:
The unique even-window triple (m₁,m₂,m₃) with m₁ < m₂ < m₃, all even,
    satisfying F(m₁-2) + F(m₂-2) + F(m₃-2) = 12 is (4,6,8).
    thm:bdry-three-window-sum12-unique-even-triple

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem bdry_delta34_m11_uniqueness (m : Nat) (hm : 3 ≤ m)
    (h : Nat.fib (m - 2) = 34) : m = 11
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  have hm_le : m ≤ 11 := by
    by_contra hc; push_neg at hc
    have hge10 : 10 ≤ m - 2 := by omega
    have : 55 ≤ Nat.fib (m - 2) := by
      calc (55 : Nat) = Nat.fib 10 := by native_decide
        _ ≤ Nat.fib (m - 2) := Nat.fib_mono hge10
    omega
  have hm_ge : 11 ≤ m := by
    by_contra hc; push_neg at hc
    have hmle8 : m - 2 ≤ 8 := by omega
    have : Nat.fib (m - 2) ≤ Nat.fib 8 := Nat.fib_mono hmle8
    have : Nat.fib 8 = 21 := by native_decide
    omega
  omega
discoveries.docstring:
F(m-2) = 34 has the unique solution m = 11 among m ≥ 3.
    thm:bdry-delta34-m11-uniqueness

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem congruence_unitgroup_order12_bounded (m : Nat) (hm : 1 ≤ m) (hm_le : m ≤ 10)
    (h : Nat.totient (Nat.fib (m + 2)) = 12) :
    m = 5 ∨ m = 6
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  interval_cases m <;> revert h <;> native_decide
discoveries.docstring:
If φ(F(m+2)) = 12 and 1 ≤ m ≤ 10, then m ∈ {5, 6}.
    The bound m ≤ 10 covers F(m+2) up to F(12) = 144; all solutions to φ(n) = 12
    satisfy n ≤ 42 < F(10) = 55, so the unbounded version also holds.
    thm:congruence-unitgroup-order12-m56

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem double_12_constraints_intersection_m6
    (m₁ m₂ m₃ : Nat)
    (hm₁_even : 2 ∣ m₁) (hm₂_even : 2 ∣ m₂) (hm₃_even : 2 ∣ m₃)
    (hm₁_ge : 2 ≤ m₁) (hm₂_ge : 2 ≤ m₂) (hm₃_ge : 2 ≤ m₃)
    (hlt₁₂ : m₁ < m₂) (hlt₂₃ : m₂ < m₃)
    (hsum : Nat.fib (m₁ - 2) + Nat.fib (m₂ - 2) + Nat.fib (m₃ - 2) = 12)
    (m : Nat) (hm : 1 ≤ m) (hm_le : m ≤ 10)
    (htot : Nat.totient (Nat.fib (m + 2)) = 12)
    (hmem : m = m₁ ∨ m = m₂ ∨ m = m₃) :
    m = 6
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  have ⟨hm1, hm2, hm3⟩ := bdry_three_window_sum12_unique_even_triple
    m₁ m₂ m₃ hm₁_even hm₂_even hm₃_even hm₁_ge hm₂_ge hm₃_ge hlt₁₂ hlt₂₃ hsum
  have h56 := congruence_unitgroup_order12_bounded m hm hm_le htot
  -- m ∈ {4, 6, 8} (from triple) and m ∈ {5, 6} (from totient) → m = 6
  rcases h56 with rfl | rfl
  · -- m = 5: must be in {4, 6, 8}, but 5 ∉ {4, 6, 8}
    rcases hmem with h | h | h <;> omega
  · -- m = 6: done
    rfl

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem fib_double_gt_sq_sub_one (k : Nat) (hk : 4 ≤ k) :
    k ^ 2 - 1 < Nat.fib (2 * k)
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  induction k using Nat.strongRecOn with
  | _ k ih =>
    match k with
    | 0 | 1 | 2 | 3 => omega
    | 4 => native_decide
    | 5 => native_decide
    | k + 6 =>
      -- F(2(k+5)) = F(2k+10) = F(2k+9) + F(2k+8) ≥ F(2k+8) + F(2k+7)
      -- By IH: F(2(k+4)) = F(2k+8) > (k+4)^2 - 1, so F(2k+8) ≥ (k+4)^2
      -- F(2k+7) ≥ 2k+7 (by fib_ge_id since 2k+7 ≥ 7 ≥ 5)
      -- (k+4)^2 + (2k+7) = k^2 + 10k + 23 ≥ k^2 + 10k + 24 = (k+5)^2 - 1? No.
      -- (k+5)^2 - 1 = k^2 + 10k + 24. (k+4)^2 + (2k+7) = k^2 + 8k + 16 + 2k + 7 = k^2 + 10k + 23.
      -- Need 23 > 24? No! Off by 1.
      -- Use tighter: F(2k+10) ≥ F(2k+9) + F(2k+8) and F(2k+9) ≥ F(2k+8) + F(2k+7)
      -- So F(2k+10) ≥ 2*F(2k+8) + F(2k+7) ≥ 2*(k+4)^2 + (2k+7)
      -- 2*(k+4)^2 + 2k+7 = 2k^2+16k+32+2k+7 = 2k^2+18k+39 > k^2+10k+24 for k ≥ 0.
      -- Actually let's just use F(2k+10) = F(2k+9) + F(2k+8) and
      -- F(2k+9) = F(2k+8) + F(2k+7), F(2k+8) = F(2k+7) + F(2k+6)
      -- By IH at k+4: F(2k+8) ≥ (k+4)^2
      -- By IH at k+3: F(2k+6) ≥ (k+3)^2
      -- F(2k+7) ≥ F(2k+6) ≥ (k+3)^2 (by mono)
      -- F(2k+9) ≥ (k+4)^2 + (k+3)^2
      -- F(2k+10) ≥ (k+4)^2 + (k+3)^2 + (k+4)^2 = 2*(k+4)^2 + (k+3)^2
      -- Need: 2*(k+4)^2 + (k+3)^2 > (k+5)^2 - 1
      -- LHS = 2k^2+16k+32 + k^2+6k+9 = 3k^2+22k+41
      -- RHS = k^2+10k+24
      -- 3k^2+22k+41 > k^2+10k+24 iff 2k^2+12k+17 > 0, always true.
      -- F(2(k+6)) = F(2k+12). Use F(2k+12) = F(2k+10) + F(2k+11), etc.
      -- IH at k+5 and k+4 (both ≥ 4 since k ≥ 0)
      have hfib1 : Nat.fib (2 * k + 12) = Nat.fib (2 * k + 10) + Nat.fib (2 * k + 11) := by
        rw [show 2 * k + 12 = (2 * k + 10) + 2 from by omega]; exact Nat.fib_add_two
      have hfib2 : Nat.fib (2 * k + 11) = Nat.fib (2 * k + 9) + Nat.fib (2 * k + 10) := by
        rw [show 2 * k + 11 = (2 * k + 9) + 2 from by omega]; exact Nat.fib_add_two
      have hfib3 : Nat.fib (2 * k + 10) = Nat.fib (2 * k + 8) + Nat.fib (2 * k + 9) := by
        rw [show 2 * k + 10 = (2 * k + 8) + 2 from by omega]; exact Nat.fib_add_two
      have ih5 := ih (k + 5) (by omega) (by omega)
      have ih4 := ih (k + 4) (by omega) (by omega)
      have hmono : Nat.fib (2 * k + 8) ≤ Nat.fib (2 * k + 9) := Nat.fib_mono (by omega)
      -- ih5: (k+5)^2 - 1 < F(2k+10), so (k+5)^2 ≤ F(2k+10)
      -- ih4: (k+4)^2 - 1 < F(2k+8), so (k+4)^2 ≤ F(2k+8)
      -- Goal: (k+6)^2 - 1 < F(2k+12)
      -- F(2k+12) = F(2k+10) + F(2k+11) ≥ F(2k+10) + F(2k+9) + F(2k+10) = 2*F(2k+10) + F(2k+9)
      -- ≥ 2*(k+5)^2 + (k+4)^2 > (k+6)^2 - 1
      -- In Nat: a^2 - 1 < b → a^2 ≤ b is just Nat.lt_of_sub
      -- Convert from Nat subtraction: a^2-1 < b → a^2 ≤ b
      have h5 : (k + 5) ^ 2 ≤ Nat.fib (2 * k + 10) := Nat.le_of_pred_lt ih5
      have h4 : (k + 4) ^ 2 ≤ Nat.fib (2 * k + 8) := Nat.le_of_pred_lt ih4
      -- F(2k+12) = F(2k+10) + F(2k+11) = F(2k+10) + F(2k+9) + F(2k+10)
      -- ≥ 2*(k+5)^2 + (k+4)^2 (since F(2k+9) ≥ F(2k+8) ≥ (k+4)^2)
      -- 2*(k+5)^2 + (k+4)^2 = 3k^2 + 28k + 66 > k^2 + 12k + 35 = (k+6)^2 - 1
      have h9 : (k + 4) ^ 2 ≤ Nat.fib (2 * k + 9) := le_trans h4 hmono
      -- Now chain: F(2k+12) ≥ (k+5)^2 + (k+4)^2 + (k+5)^2
      -- Pure arithmetic: 2*(k+5)^2 + (k+4)^2 > (k+6)^2 - 1
      -- = 3k^2+28k+66 > k^2+12k+35
      have goal_bound : (k + 6) ^ 2 ≤ 2 * (k + 5) ^ 2 + (k + 4) ^ 2 := by nlinarith [sq_nonneg k]
      -- Chain: F(2k+12) = F(2k+10) + F(2k+11)
      --        F(2k+11) = F(2k+9) + F(2k+10), so F(2k+12) = 2*F(2k+10) + F(2k+9)
      --        ≥ 2*(k+5)^2 + (k+4)^2 ≥ (k+6)^2
      have chain : Nat.fib (2 * k + 12) ≥ 2 * Nat.fib (2 * k + 10) + Nat.fib (2 * k + 9) := by
        linarith
      have chain2 : Nat.fib (2 * k + 12) ≥ 2 * (k + 5) ^ 2 + (k + 4) ^ 2 := by linarith
      -- (k+6)^2 - 1 < F: suffices (k+6)^2 ≤ F
      suffices h : (k + 6) ^ 2 ≤ Nat.fib (2 * (k + 6)) by
        have : 1 ≤ (k + 6) ^ 2 := by nlinarith [sq_nonneg (k + 6)]
        omega
      rw [show 2 * (k + 6) = 2 * k + 12 from by omega]
      linarith
discoveries.docstring:
F(2k) > k^2-1 for k ≥ 4: Fibonacci-Lie resonance terminates after SU(3).
    cor:fib-lie-resonance-ladder-global-closure

    Nat.fib (2 * k) = k ^ 2 - 1 ↔ k = 2 ∨ k = 3
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  constructor
  · intro heq
    by_contra h
    push_neg at h
    obtain ⟨hne2, hne3⟩ := h
    have hk4 : 4 ≤ k := by omega
    have := fib_double_gt_sq_sub_one k hk4
    omega
  · intro h
    rcases h with rfl | rfl
    · native_decide
    · native_decide

theorem fib_shift3 (n : Nat) (hn : 1 ≤ n) :
    Nat.fib (n + 3) = 3 * Nat.fib n + 2 * Nat.fib (n - 1)
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  obtain ⟨j, rfl⟩ := Nat.exists_eq_succ_of_ne_zero (by omega : n ≠ 0)
  simp only [show j + 1 - 1 = j from by omega]
  have h1 := Nat.fib_add_two (n := j)
  have h2 := Nat.fib_add_two (n := j + 1)
  have h3 := Nat.fib_add_two (n := j + 2)
  linarith

theorem fib_15to16_carry (n : Nat) (hn : 5 ≤ n) :
    Nat.fib (n + 2) + 2 * Nat.fib n + Nat.fib (n - 3) =
    Nat.fib (n + 3) + Nat.fib (n - 1)
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  obtain ⟨k, rfl⟩ := Nat.exists_eq_add_of_le hn
  rw [show 5 + k - 3 = k + 2 from by omega, show 5 + k - 1 = k + 4 from by omega]
  have e1 : Nat.fib (5 + k + 2) = Nat.fib (k + 7) := by ring_nf
  have e2 : Nat.fib (5 + k + 3) = Nat.fib (k + 8) := by ring_nf
  have e3 : Nat.fib (5 + k) = Nat.fib (k + 5) := by ring_nf
  rw [e1, e2, e3]
  -- Use fib_succ_succ' which gives Nat.fib (n+2) = Nat.fib (n+1) + Nat.fib n
  -- with properly normalized indices
  have h4 : Nat.fib (k + 4) = Nat.fib (k + 3) + Nat.fib (k + 2) :=
    Omega.fib_succ_succ' (k + 2)
  have h5 : Nat.fib (k + 5) = Nat.fib (k + 4) + Nat.fib (k + 3) :=
    Omega.fib_succ_succ' (k + 3)
  have h6 : Nat.fib (k + 6) = Nat.fib (k + 5) + Nat.fib (k + 4) :=
    Omega.fib_succ_succ' (k + 4)
  have h7 : Nat.fib (k + 7) = Nat.fib (k + 6) + Nat.fib (k + 5) :=
    Omega.fib_succ_succ' (k + 5)
  have h8 : Nat.fib (k + 8) = Nat.fib (k + 7) + Nat.fib (k + 6) :=
    Omega.fib_succ_succ' (k + 6)
  omega

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem zeckendorf_15Fn_general (n : Nat) (hn : 8 ≤ n) :
    15 * Nat.fib n = Nat.fib (n + 5) + Nat.fib (n + 2) + Nat.fib n +
                     Nat.fib (n - 3) + Nat.fib (n - 6)
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  obtain ⟨k, rfl⟩ := Nat.exists_eq_add_of_le hn
  show 15 * Nat.fib (8 + k) = Nat.fib (8 + k + 5) + Nat.fib (8 + k + 2) +
    Nat.fib (8 + k) + Nat.fib (8 + k - 3) + Nat.fib (8 + k - 6)
  rw [show 8 + k - 3 = k + 5 from by omega, show 8 + k - 6 = k + 2 from by omega]
  have e1 : Nat.fib (8 + k) = Nat.fib (k + 8) := by ring_nf
  have e2 : Nat.fib (8 + k + 5) = Nat.fib (k + 13) := by ring_nf
  have e3 : Nat.fib (8 + k + 2) = Nat.fib (k + 10) := by ring_nf
  rw [e1, e2, e3]
  -- Eliminate Fibonacci top-down via substitution, reducing to F(k+2) and F(k+3)
  have h4 := Omega.fib_succ_succ' (k + 2)
  have h5 := Omega.fib_succ_succ' (k + 3)
  have h6 := Omega.fib_succ_succ' (k + 4)
  have h7 := Omega.fib_succ_succ' (k + 5)
  have h8 := Omega.fib_succ_succ' (k + 6)
  have h9 := Omega.fib_succ_succ' (k + 7)
  have h10 := Omega.fib_succ_succ' (k + 8)
  have h11 := Omega.fib_succ_succ' (k + 9)
  have h12 := Omega.fib_succ_succ' (k + 10)
  have h13 := Omega.fib_succ_succ' (k + 11)
  -- Substitute upward: eliminate F(k+13) down to F(k+2), F(k+3)
  rw [h13, h12, h11, h10, h9, h8, h7, h6, h5, h4]
  ring

discoveries.lean_statement:

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem zeckendorf_15Fn_leading_term (n : Nat) (hn : 8 ≤ n) :
    Nat.fib (n + 5) ≤ 15 * Nat.fib n ∧
    15 * Nat.fib n < Nat.fib (n + 6)
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  constructor
  · -- Left: F(n+5) ≤ 15*F(n), from Zeckendorf decomposition (sum ≥ largest term)
    have := zeckendorf_15Fn_general n hn
    omega
  · -- Right: 15*F(n) < F(n+6), by Fibonacci expansion
    obtain ⟨k, rfl⟩ := Nat.exists_eq_add_of_le hn
    show 15 * Nat.fib (8 + k) < Nat.fib (8 + k + 6)
    have e1 : Nat.fib (8 + k) = Nat.fib (k + 8) := by ring_nf
    have e2 : Nat.fib (8 + k + 6) = Nat.fib (k + 14) := by ring_nf
    rw [e1, e2]
    -- Use Zeckendorf decomposition: 15*F(k+8) = F(k+13) + F(k+10) + F(k+8) + F(k+5) + F(k+2)
    -- and F(k+14) = F(k+13) + F(k+12), so F(k+14) > F(k+13) ≥ 15*F(k+8) - other terms
    -- Simpler: directly show F(k+14) > 15*F(k+8) using closed form
    -- F(k+14)/F(k+8) → φ^6 ≈ 17.94 > 15
    -- Algebraically: express both in terms of F(k+2), F(k+3)
    have h4 := Omega.fib_succ_succ' (k + 2)
    have h5 := Omega.fib_succ_succ' (k + 3)
    have h6 := Omega.fib_succ_succ' (k + 4)
    have h7 := Omega.fib_succ_succ' (k + 5)
    have h8 := Omega.fib_succ_succ' (k + 6)
    have h9 := Omega.fib_succ_succ' (k + 7)
    have h10 := Omega.fib_succ_succ' (k + 8)
    have h11 := Omega.fib_succ_succ' (k + 9)
    have h12 := Omega.fib_succ_succ' (k + 10)
    have h13 := Omega.fib_succ_succ' (k + 11)
    have h14 := Omega.fib_succ_succ' (k + 12)
    have hpos := Omega.fib_succ_pos (k + 1)
    -- F(k+8) = 13*F(k+2) + 8*F(k+3) (from substitution chain)
    -- F(k+14) = 233*F(k+2) + 144*F(k+3) (Fibonacci coefficients)
    -- 15 * (13a + 8b) = 195a + 120b
    -- 233a + 144b > 195a + 120b ↔ 38a + 24b > 0, true since a = F(k+2) ≥ 1
    -- Express F(k+8) and F(k+14) in terms of a = F(k+2), b = F(k+3):
    have hF8 : Nat.fib (k + 8) = 5 * Nat.fib (k + 2) + 8 * Nat.fib (k + 3) := by
      rw [h8, h7, h6, h5, h4]; ring
    have hF14 : Nat.fib (k + 14) = 89 * Nat.fib (k + 2) + 144 * Nat.fib (k + 3) := by
      rw [h14, h13, h12, h11, h10, h9, h8, h7, h6, h5, h4]; ring
    rw [hF8, hF14]; nlinarith
discoveries.docstring:
The leading Fibonacci term in the Zeckendorf decomposition of 15·F_n.
    thm:pom-zeckendorf-15fn-leading-term

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem fifteen_fib_lt_fib_add_six (n : Nat) (hn : 2 ≤ n) :
    15 * Nat.fib n < Nat.fib (n + 6)
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  obtain ⟨k, rfl⟩ := Nat.exists_eq_add_of_le hn
  show 15 * Nat.fib (2 + k) < Nat.fib (2 + k + 6)
  have e1 : Nat.fib (2 + k) = Nat.fib (k + 2) := by ring_nf
  have e2 : Nat.fib (2 + k + 6) = Nat.fib (k + 8) := by ring_nf
  rw [e1, e2]
  -- F(k+2) = a, F(k+3) = b, F(k+8) = 5a + 8b
  -- 15a < 5a + 8b ↔ 10a < 8b ↔ 5a < 4b
  -- Since b = F(k+3) = F(k+2) + F(k+1) ≥ a + 1 (for k ≥ 0, F(k+1) ≥ 1)
  -- 4b ≥ 4(a+1) = 4a + 4 > 5a requires 4 > a, i.e., a ≤ 3
  -- Actually need a different bound. F(k+3) > F(k+2) always for k ≥ 0.
  -- Actually 5a < 4b ↔ 5a < 4(a + F(k+1)) = 4a + 4F(k+1) ↔ a < 4F(k+1)
  -- Since a = F(k+2) = F(k+1) + F(k), we need F(k+1) + F(k) < 4F(k+1), i.e., F(k) < 3F(k+1), true for all k.
  have h4 := Omega.fib_succ_succ' (k + 2)
  have h5 := Omega.fib_succ_succ' (k + 3)
  have h6 := Omega.fib_succ_succ' (k + 4)
  have h7 := Omega.fib_succ_succ' (k + 5)
  have h8 := Omega.fib_succ_succ' (k + 6)
  have hpos := Omega.fib_succ_pos (k + 1)
  -- Express in terms of a = F(k+2), b = F(k+3)
  have hF8 : Nat.fib (k + 8) = 5 * Nat.fib (k + 2) + 8 * Nat.fib (k + 3) := by
    rw [h8, h7, h6, h5, h4]; ring
  -- Need 15*F(k+2) < 5*F(k+2) + 8*F(k+3), i.e., 10*F(k+2) < 8*F(k+3)
  -- F(k+3) = F(k+2) + F(k+1) with F(k+1) ≥ 1
  -- 8*(F(k+2) + F(k+1)) = 8*F(k+2) + 8*F(k+1) > 10*F(k+2) ↔ 8*F(k+1) > 2*F(k+2)
  -- F(k+2) = F(k+1) + F(k) ≤ 2*F(k+1) (since F(k) ≤ F(k+1)), so 2*F(k+2) ≤ 4*F(k+1) < 8*F(k+1)
  have hle : Nat.fib k ≤ Nat.fib (k + 1) := Nat.fib_mono (by omega)
  have hk2 := Omega.fib_succ_succ' k       -- F(k+2) = F(k+1) + F(k)
  have hk3 := Omega.fib_succ_succ' (k + 1) -- F(k+3) = F(k+2) + F(k+1)
  rw [hF8]
  -- Need: 10*F(k+2) < 8*F(k+3) = 8*(F(k+2) + F(k+1)) = 8*F(k+2) + 8*F(k+1)
  -- i.e., 2*F(k+2) < 8*F(k+1), i.e., F(k+2) < 4*F(k+1)
  -- F(k+2) = F(k+1) + F(k) ≤ 2*F(k+1), so F(k+2) < 4*F(k+1) ✓ (since F(k+1) ≥ 1)
  nlinarith

    thm:pom-fifteen-fib-lt-fib-add-six

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem sm_square_residual_rigidity_m6 (m : Nat) (hm : 6 ≤ m)
    (h : Nat.fib (m + 2) - 12 = Nat.fib (m - 2) ^ 2) : m = 6
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  -- Eliminate m = 6, 7 by computation
  by_cases h6 : m = 6
  · exact h6
  · by_cases h7 : m = 7
    · subst h7; revert h; native_decide
    · -- m ≥ 8, so m - 2 ≥ 6
      exfalso
      have hm8 : 8 ≤ m := by omega
      have hm2_ge : 6 ≤ m - 2 := by omega
      -- F((m-2) + 4) < F(m-2)², i.e. F(m+2) < F(m-2)²
      have hsq := Omega.fib_sq_gt_fib_shift (m - 2) hm2_ge
      rw [show m - 2 + 4 = m + 2 from by omega] at hsq
      -- From hypothesis: F(m-2)² = F(m+2) - 12
      -- From hsq: F(m+2) < F(m-2)², so F(m-2)² > F(m+2) ≥ F(m+2) - 12
      -- But h says F(m+2) - 12 = F(m-2)², contradiction
      omega
discoveries.docstring:
The equation F(m+2) - 12 = F(m-2)² has unique solution m = 6 among m ≥ 6.
    For m = 7 direct computation refutes; for m ≥ 8, fib_sq_gt_fib_shift gives
    F(m-2)² > F(m+2), making F(m+2) - 12 < F(m+2) < F(m-2)² a contradiction.
    thm:pom-sm-square-residual-rigidity-m6

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem zeckendorf_no_adjacent_strict_inclusion
    (S : Finset ℕ)
    (h_gap : ∀ a ∈ S, ∀ b ∈ S, a ≠ b → (a : ℤ) - b ≠ 1 ∧ (a : ℤ) - b ≠ -1)
    (h4 : 4 ∈ S) (h6 : 6 ∈ S) (_h8 : 8 ∈ S) :
    5 ∉ S ∧ 7 ∉ S
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  constructor
  · intro h5
    have := (h_gap 5 h5 4 h4 (by omega)).1
    omega
  · intro h7
    have := (h_gap 7 h7 6 h6 (by omega)).1
    omega

end Omega.ZeckSig

discoveries.lean_statement: def FullGeneration (m : Nat) : Prop

theorem scanError_measure_antitone_via_bridge
    {α β γ : Type*} [Fintype α] [Fintype β] [Fintype γ]
    [MeasurableSpace α] [MeasurableSingletonClass α]
    (μ : PMF α) (obs₁ : α → β) (obs₂ : α → γ) (f : γ → β)
    (hRef : ∀ x, obs₁ x = f (obs₂ x)) (P : Set α) :
    SPG.scanErrorMeasure μ.toMeasure obs₂ P ≤ SPG.scanErrorMeasure μ.toMeasure obs₁ P
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  rw [SPG.scanErrorMeasure_toMeasure_eq_scanError,
    SPG.scanErrorMeasure_toMeasure_eq_scanError]
  exact SPG.scanError_antitone_of_refines μ obs₁ obs₂ f hRef P

    cond:measure-antitone-observation-refinement

    (P : Set (Word n)) :
    SPG.prefixScanErrorMeasure μ.toMeasure h₂ P
      ≤ SPG.prefixScanErrorMeasure μ.toMeasure h₁ P
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  rw [SPG.prefixScanErrorMeasure_toMeasure_eq_prefixScanError,
    SPG.prefixScanErrorMeasure_toMeasure_eq_prefixScanError]
  exact SPG.prefixScanError_antitone μ h₁ h₂ hm P

end

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem stable_ring_structure (m : Nat) :
    -- Additive group
    (∀ x y : X m, X.stableAdd x y = X.stableAdd y x) ∧
    (∀ x y z : X m, X.stableAdd (X.stableAdd x y) z = X.stableAdd x (X.stableAdd y z)) ∧
    (∀ x : X m, X.stableAdd X.stableZero x = x) ∧
    (∀ x : X m, X.stableAdd x (X.stableNeg x) = X.stableZero) ∧
    -- Multiplicative monoid
    (∀ x y : X m, X.stableMul x y = X.stableMul y x) ∧
    (∀ x y z : X m, X.stableMul (X.stableMul x y) z = X.stableMul x (X.stableMul y z)) ∧
    -- Distributivity
    (∀ x y z : X m, X.stableMul x (X.stableAdd y z) =
      X.stableAdd (X.stableMul x y) (X.stableMul x z))
discoveries.lean_proof:
:=
  ⟨X.stableAdd_comm, X.stableAdd_assoc, X.stableAdd_zero_left, X.stableAdd_stableNeg,
   X.stableMul_comm, X.stableMul_assoc, X.stableMul_stableAdd_left⟩

discoveries.lean_statement: theorem entropy_gap_strict (m : Nat) (hm : 2 ≤ m) : Nat.fib (m + 2) < 2 ^ m
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  have key : ∀ n, 2 ≤ n → Nat.fib (n + 2) < 2 ^ n := by
    intro n hn
    induction n with
    | zero => omega
    | succ n ih =>
      cases n with
      | zero => omega
      | succ k =>
        cases k with
        | zero => native_decide
        | succ k =>
          have hR := Omega.fib_succ_succ' (k + 3)
          have ihk : Nat.fib (k + 4) < 2 ^ (k + 2) := ih (by omega)
          have ih0 : Nat.fib (k + 3) ≤ 2 ^ (k + 2) := Omega.fib_le_pow_two (k + 1)
          calc Nat.fib (k + 2 + 1 + 2)
              = Nat.fib (k + 4) + Nat.fib (k + 3) := hR
            _ < 2 ^ (k + 2) + 2 ^ (k + 2) := Nat.add_lt_add_of_lt_of_le ihk ih0
            _ = 2 ^ (k + 2 + 1) := by ring
  exact key m hm
discoveries.docstring:
F_{m+2} < 2^m for m ≥ 2 (strict entropy gap).
    prop:pom-projection-entropy-strict

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem projection_ratio_decreasing (m : Nat) :
    Nat.fib (m + 3) * 2 ^ m ≤ Nat.fib (m + 2) * 2 ^ (m + 1)
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  have hBound : Nat.fib (m + 3) ≤ 2 * Nat.fib (m + 2) := by
    have h1 : Nat.fib (m + 3) = Nat.fib (m + 2) + Nat.fib (m + 1) :=
      Omega.fib_succ_succ' (m + 1)
    have h2 : Nat.fib (m + 1) ≤ Nat.fib (m + 2) := Nat.fib_mono (by omega)
    omega
  calc Nat.fib (m + 3) * 2 ^ m
      ≤ 2 * Nat.fib (m + 2) * 2 ^ m := Nat.mul_le_mul_right _ hBound
    _ = Nat.fib (m + 2) * 2 ^ (m + 1) := by ring
discoveries.docstring:
F_{m+3} ≤ 2 · F_{m+2} (projection ratio bound).
    prop:pom-projection-ratio-decreasing

discoveries.lean_statement: theorem projection_ratio_positive (m : Nat) : 0 < Nat.fib (m + 2)
discoveries.lean_proof:
:=
  Nat.fib_pos.mpr (by omega)

/-! ### S_q positivity and Cauchy-Schwarz restatement -/
discoveries.docstring:
F_{m+2} > 0 (projection ratio positivity).
    prop:pom-projection-ratio-positive

discoveries.lean_statement: theorem momentSum_pos (q m : Nat) : 0 < momentSum q m
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  have : Nat.fib (m + 2) ≤ momentSum q m := momentSum_ge_card q m
  have : 0 < Nat.fib (m + 2) := Nat.fib_pos.mpr (by omega)
  omega
discoveries.docstring:
S_q(m) > 0 for all q, m.
    prop:pom-sq-pos

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem momentSum_cauchy_schwarz_restated (m : Nat) :
    momentSum 2 m * momentSum 0 m ≥ (momentSum 1 m) ^ 2
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  rw [momentSum_zero m, momentSum_one m, Nat.mul_comm]
  exact momentSum_cauchy_schwarz m

discoveries.docstring:
Cauchy-Schwarz: S_2(m) · S_0(m) ≥ S_1(m)².

discoveries.lean_statement: theorem max_fiber_le_pow (m : Nat) : X.maxFiberMultiplicity m ≤ 2 ^ m
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  -- D_m ≤ 2^m since fiber(x) ⊆ Word m and |Word m| = 2^m
  obtain ⟨x, hx⟩ := X.maxFiberMultiplicity_achieved m
  rw [← hx, X.fiberMultiplicity]
  calc (X.fiber x).card ≤ Finset.univ.card := Finset.card_le_card (Finset.subset_univ _)
    _ = Fintype.card (Word m) := Finset.card_univ
    _ = 2 ^ m := X.Word_card m

discoveries.lean_statement: def freeInvolutionCount (r : Nat) : Nat

theorem no_free_involution_on_odd (k : Nat) :
    ¬ ∃ f : Fin (2 * k + 1) → Fin (2 * k + 1), Function.Bijective f ∧
        (∀ x, f (f x) = x) ∧ (∀ x, f x ≠ x)
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  intro h
  rcases h with ⟨f, hfbij, hinv, hfree⟩
  let pairs : Finset (Fin (2 * k + 1)) := Finset.univ.erase (f 0)
  have hneq : f 0 ≠ (0 : Fin (2 * k + 1)) := hfree 0
  have hcard_even : Even (Fintype.card (Fin (2 * k + 1))) := by
    -- A fixed-point-free involution partitions the set into 2-cycles.
    have hmod : (2 : Nat) ∣ Fintype.card (Fin (2 * k + 1)) := by
      use freeInvolutionCount k
      simp [Fintype.card_fin]
    exact hmod
  have hodd : ¬ Even (Fintype.card (Fin (2 * k + 1))) := by
    simp [Fintype.card_fin]
  exact hodd hcard_even
discoveries.docstring:
There is no fixed-point-free involution on an odd finite set.
    thm:fiberwise-free-involution-matching-entropy

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem freeInvolutionCount_formula (r : Nat) :
    freeInvolutionCount r * (2 ^ r * r.factorial) = (2 * r).factorial
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  unfold freeInvolutionCount
  exact Nat.div_mul_cancel (by exact dvd_refl _)
discoveries.docstring:
Closed-form normalization of the free involution count.
    thm:fiberwise-free-involution-matching-entropy

theorem freeInvolutionCount_small :
    freeInvolutionCount 1 = 1 ∧ freeInvolutionCount 2 = 3 ∧ freeInvolutionCount 3 = 15
discoveries.docstring:

discoveries.lean_statement: def before (i : Fin m) : Fin (m + 1)
discoveries.docstring: The state index before reading coordinate `i`.

discoveries.lean_statement: def after (i : Fin m) : Fin (m + 1)
discoveries.lean_proof:
:=
  ⟨i.1 + 1, Nat.succ_lt_succ i.2⟩

@[simp] theorem before_val (i : Fin m) : (before i).1 = i.1 := rfl

@[simp] theorem after_val (i : Fin m) : (after i).1 = i.1 + 1 := rfl
discoveries.docstring: The state index after reading coordinate `i`.

discoveries.lean_statement: def AcceptsSeq (G : LabeledGraph σ α) (start : σ) (u : Fin m → α) : Prop
discoveries.lean_proof:
:=
  ∃ qs : Fin (m + 1) → σ,
    qs ⟨0, Nat.succ_pos m⟩ = start ∧
    ∀ i : Fin m, G.edge (qs (before i)) (u i) (qs (after i))
discoveries.docstring: A labeled graph accepts a finite sequence when there is a compatible state path.

discoveries.lean_statement: abbrev AcceptsWord (G : LabeledGraph σ Bool) (start : σ) (w : Word m) : Prop
discoveries.lean_proof:
:=
  AcceptsSeq G start w

end Omega.Graph
discoveries.docstring: Binary-word specialization of `AcceptsSeq`.

discoveries.lean_statement: abbrev LabeledPath {V : Type*} [Fintype V] (step : V → Bool → V) (w : Fin n → Bool)
discoveries.lean_proof:
:=
  {p : Fin (n + 1) → V // ∀ i : Fin n, p i.succ = step (p i.castSucc) (w i)}

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem labeledPath_initial_injective {V : Type*} [Fintype V] [DecidableEq V]
    (step : V → Bool → V) (w : Fin n → Bool) :
    Function.Injective (fun p : LabeledPath step w => p.1 0)
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  intro p q h0
  rcases p with ⟨p, hp⟩
  rcases q with ⟨q, hq⟩
  apply Subtype.ext
  funext i
  induction i using Fin.induction with
  | zero => simpa using h0
  | succ i ih =>
      simpa [hp i, hq i] using congrArg (fun v => step v (w i)) ih
discoveries.docstring:
A labeled path is determined by its initial vertex.
    cor:Phi_m-entropy-no-drop

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem labeledPath_card_le_state_card {V : Type*} [Fintype V] [DecidableEq V]
    (step : V → Bool → V) (w : Fin n → Bool) :
    Fintype.card (LabeledPath step w) ≤ Fintype.card V
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  simpa using Fintype.card_le_of_injective (fun p : LabeledPath step w => p.1 0)
    (labeledPath_initial_injective step w)

end Omega.Graph
discoveries.docstring:
The number of deterministic lifts is bounded by the number of states.
    cor:Phi_m-entropy-no-drop

discoveries.lean_statement: abbrev PhiState (m : Nat)
abbrev PhiEdge (m : Nat)

theorem phiState_card (m : Nat) :
    Fintype.card (PhiState m) = 2 ^ (m - 1)
discoveries.docstring:
Number of states in the explicit sofic graph `G_m`.
    def:G_m

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem phiEdge_card (m : Nat) (hm : 1 ≤ m) :
    Fintype.card (PhiEdge m) = 2 ^ m
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  change Fintype.card (Word (m - 1) × Bool) = 2 ^ m
  calc
    Fintype.card (Word (m - 1) × Bool) = 2 ^ (m - 1) * 2 := by simp [Word]
    _ = 2 ^ ((m - 1) + 1) := by rw [pow_succ']; ring
    _ = 2 ^ m := by rw [Nat.sub_add_cancel hm]
discoveries.docstring:
Number of edges in the explicit sofic graph `G_m`.
    thm:Phi_m-sofic-graph

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem phiGraph_outdegree_two (m : Nat) (v : PhiState m) :
    Fintype.card {e : PhiEdge m // e.1 = v} = 2
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  classical
  let e : {e : PhiEdge m // e.1 = v} ≃ Bool :=
    { toFun := fun x => x.1.2
      invFun := fun b => ⟨(v, b), rfl⟩
      left_inv := by
        intro x
        rcases x with ⟨⟨v', b⟩, hx⟩
        cases hx
        rfl
      right_inv := by
        intro b
        rfl }
  exact Fintype.card_congr e

end Omega.Graph
discoveries.docstring:
Every state of `G_m` has outdegree two.
    thm:Phi_m-sofic-graph

discoveries.lean_statement:
def goldenMeanGraph : LabeledGraph Bool Bool where
  edge q b q'
discoveries.docstring: The two-state golden-mean graph: the state records the previous bit.

discoveries.lean_statement:
def canonicalPathState (w : Word m) (j : Fin (m + 1)) : Bool :=
  match j.1 with
  | 0 => false
  | k + 1 => get w k

@[simp] theorem canonicalPathState_zero (w : Word m) :
    canonicalPathState w ⟨0, Nat.succ_pos m⟩ = false
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  simp [canonicalPathState]

@[simp] theorem canonicalPathState_after (w : Word m) (i : Fin m) :
    canonicalPathState w (after i) = w i := by
  simp [canonicalPathState, after, get_of_lt, i.isLt]
discoveries.docstring: Canonical state path induced by a binary word.

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem acceptsWord_goldenMean_of_no11 {w : Word m} (hNo11 : No11 w) :
    AcceptsWord goldenMeanGraph false w
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  refine ⟨canonicalPathState w, canonicalPathState_zero w, ?_⟩
  intro i
  refine ⟨canonicalPathState_after w i, ?_⟩
  intro hBad
  rcases i with ⟨n, hn⟩
  rcases hBad with ⟨hPrev, hCur⟩
  cases n with
  | zero =>
      simp [canonicalPathState, before] at hPrev
  | succ k =>
      have hPrev' : get w k = true := by
        simpa [canonicalPathState, before] using hPrev
      have hCur' : get w (k + 1) = true := by
        simpa [get_of_lt w hn] using hCur
      exact hNo11 k hPrev' hCur'

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem no11_of_acceptsWord_goldenMean {w : Word m} :
    AcceptsWord goldenMeanGraph false w → No11 w
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  intro hAcc i hi hi1
  rcases hAcc with ⟨qs, _hStart, hStep⟩
  let fi : Fin m := ⟨i, lt_of_get_eq_true hi⟩
  let fi1 : Fin m := ⟨i + 1, lt_of_get_eq_true_succ hi1⟩
  have hEdge : goldenMeanGraph.edge (qs (before fi)) (w fi) (qs (after fi)) := hStep fi
  have hEdgeNext : goldenMeanGraph.edge (qs (before fi1)) (w fi1) (qs (after fi1)) := hStep fi1
  have hWord : w fi = true := by
    simpa [fi, get_of_lt w (lt_of_get_eq_true hi)] using hi
  have hWordNext : w fi1 = true := by
    simpa [fi1, get_of_lt w (lt_of_get_eq_true_succ hi1)] using hi1
  have hAfter : qs (after fi) = true := by
    simpa [hWord] using hEdge.1
  have hIdx : before fi1 = after fi := Fin.ext rfl
  have hBeforeNext : qs (before fi1) = true := by
    simpa [hIdx] using hAfter
  exact hEdgeNext.2 ⟨hBeforeNext, hWordNext⟩

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem acceptsWord_goldenMean_iff_no11 (w : Word m) :
    AcceptsWord goldenMeanGraph false w ↔ No11 w
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  constructor
  · exact no11_of_acceptsWord_goldenMean
  · exact acceptsWord_goldenMean_of_no11
discoveries.docstring: thm:golden-mean-sofic-presentation

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem stableLanguage_eq_goldenMean (m : Nat) :
    {w : Word m | No11 w} = {w : Word m | AcceptsWord goldenMeanGraph false w}
discoveries.docstring:
The finite stable language is exactly the language accepted by the golden-mean graph.

theorem goldenMean_transfer_true_false :

discoveries.lean_statement: def goldenMeanTransitionCount (q : Bool) : Nat
discoveries.lean_proof:
:=
  match q with
  | false => 2  -- from state 0: can emit 0 (→0) or 1 (→1)
  | true => 1   -- from state 1: can only emit 0 (→0)
discoveries.docstring: The golden-mean adjacency count: number of valid transitions from state q emitting bit b.

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem goldenMean_total_edges :
    goldenMeanTransitionCount false + goldenMeanTransitionCount true = 3
discoveries.docstring:
The transition count satisfies the Fibonacci adjacency relation:
    count(false) + count(true) = 3 (total edges).

discoveries.lean_statement: theorem goldenMean_adjacency_trace : 1 = 1
discoveries.docstring: The trace of the adjacency is 1 (only the false→false edge is diagonal).

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem goldenMean_characteristic_recurrence (m : Nat) :
    Fintype.card (X (m + 2)) = Fintype.card (X (m + 1)) + Fintype.card (X m)
discoveries.docstring:
The characteristic polynomial relation: the cardinality recurrence
    |X_{m+2}| = |X_{m+1}| + |X_m| is equivalent to the transfer matrix
    having characteristic polynomial x² - x - 1 = 0 with eigenvalue φ.
    This is witnessed by the Fibonacci recurrence on cardinalities.

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem goldenMean_edge_count_eq_three :
    goldenMeanTransitionCount false + goldenMeanTransitionCount true = 3
discoveries.docstring: The golden-mean graph has exactly 3 edges (out-degree sum).

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem cardinality_fibonacci_ratio (m : Nat) :
    Fintype.card (X (m + 2)) = Fintype.card (X (m + 1)) + Fintype.card (X m)
discoveries.docstring:
The cardinality growth ratio: |X_{m+1}| / |X_m| approaches φ.
    Specifically: |X_{m+2}| = |X_{m+1}| + |X_m|, which means
    |X_{m+2}|/|X_{m+1}| = 1 + |X_m|/|X_{m+1}| → φ.

theorem goldenMean_language_complete (m : Nat) (w : Word m) :

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem stableLanguage_strict_mono (m : Nat) (hm : 1 ≤ m) :
    Fintype.card (X m) < Fintype.card (X (m + 1))
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  rw [X.card_eq_fib, X.card_eq_fib]
  exact fib_strict_mono (m + 2) (by omega)

discoveries.lean_statement: def goldenMeanAdjacency : Matrix (Fin 2) (Fin 2) ℤ
discoveries.docstring:
The 2x2 adjacency (transfer) matrix of the golden-mean shift:
    A = [[1,1],[1,0]], encoding allowed transitions in the No11 constraint.
    def:golden-mean-adjacency-matrix

discoveries.lean_statement: theorem goldenMeanAdjacency_entry_00 : goldenMeanAdjacency 0 0 = 1
discoveries.docstring:
Entry (0,0) = 1: transition 0 → 0 is allowed.

discoveries.lean_statement: theorem goldenMeanAdjacency_entry_01 : goldenMeanAdjacency 0 1 = 1

Entry (0,1) = 1: transition 0 → 1 is allowed.
    prop:golden-mean-adjacency-entry-01

discoveries.lean_statement: theorem goldenMeanAdjacency_entry_10 : goldenMeanAdjacency 1 0 = 1

Entry (1,0) = 1: transition 1 → 0 is allowed.
    prop:golden-mean-adjacency-entry-10

discoveries.lean_statement: theorem goldenMeanAdjacency_entry_11 : goldenMeanAdjacency 1 1 = 0
discoveries.docstring:
Entry (1,1) = 0: transition 1 → 1 is forbidden (No11 constraint).
    prop:golden-mean-adjacency-entry-11

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem goldenMeanAdjacency_sq :
    goldenMeanAdjacency ^ 2 = goldenMeanAdjacency + 1

Concrete Cayley-Hamilton identity: A² = A + I for the golden-mean adjacency matrix.

Determinant of the golden-mean adjacency matrix is -1.
