discoveries.lean_statement:
def ObservablePureMeasure {α β : Type*} [MeasurableSpace α] [Fintype β]
    (μ : MeasureTheory.Measure α) (obs : α → β) (P : Set α) : Prop :=
  ∀ b, cellEventMeasure μ obs P b = 0 ∨ cellComplMeasure μ obs P b = 0

@[simp] theorem cellEventMeasure_observableEvent_of_mem {α β : Type*} [MeasurableSpace α]
    (μ : MeasureTheory.Measure α) (obs : α → β) (A : Set β) (b : β) (hb : b ∈ A) :
    cellEventMeasure μ obs (observableEvent obs A) b = cellMeasure μ obs b
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  rw [cellEventMeasure, cellMeasure, observableEvent_inter_cell obs A b hb]

@[simp] theorem cellEventMeasure_observableEvent_of_not_mem {α β : Type*} [MeasurableSpace α]
    (μ : MeasureTheory.Measure α) (obs : α → β) (A : Set β) (b : β) (hb : b ∉ A) :
    cellEventMeasure μ obs (observableEvent obs A) b = 0 := by
  rw [cellEventMeasure, observableEvent_inter_cell_of_not_mem obs A b hb, MeasureTheory.measure_empty]

@[simp] theorem cellComplMeasure_observableEvent_of_mem {α β : Type*} [MeasurableSpace α]
    (μ : MeasureTheory.Measure α) (obs : α → β) (A : Set β) (b : β) (hb : b ∈ A) :
    cellComplMeasure μ obs (observableEvent obs A) b = 0 := by
  rw [cellComplMeasure, cell_diff_observableEvent obs A b hb, MeasureTheory.measure_empty]

@[simp] theorem cellComplMeasure_observableEvent_of_not_mem {α β : Type*} [MeasurableSpace α]
    (μ : MeasureTheory.Measure α) (obs : α → β) (A : Set β) (b : β) (hb : b ∉ A) :
    cellComplMeasure μ obs (observableEvent obs A) b = cellMeasure μ obs b := by
  rw [cellComplMeasure, cellMeasure, cell_diff_observableEvent_of_not_mem obs A b hb]

Purity of an event relative to a finite observable: every observation cell is all-in or all-out.

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem scanErrorMeasure_observableEvent_eq_zero {α β : Type*} [MeasurableSpace α] [Fintype β]
    (μ : MeasureTheory.Measure α) (obs : α → β) (A : Set β) :
    scanErrorMeasure μ obs (observableEvent obs A) = 0
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  classical
  unfold scanErrorMeasure
  refine (Fintype.sum_eq_zero_iff_of_nonneg (f := fun b =>
    min (cellEventMeasure μ obs (observableEvent obs A) b)
      (cellComplMeasure μ obs (observableEvent obs A) b)) ?_).2 ?_
  · intro b
    exact bot_le
  · funext b
    by_cases hb : b ∈ A
    · simp [hb]
    · simp [hb]
discoveries.docstring: thm:spg-measure-observable-zero-error

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem observablePureMeasure_observableEvent {α β : Type*} [MeasurableSpace α] [Fintype β]
    (μ : MeasureTheory.Measure α) (obs : α → β) (A : Set β) :
    ObservablePureMeasure μ obs (observableEvent obs A)
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  intro b
  by_cases hb : b ∈ A
  · right
    simp [hb]
  · left
    simp [hb]

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem scanErrorMeasure_eq_zero_of_observablePure {α β : Type*} [MeasurableSpace α] [Fintype β]
    (μ : MeasureTheory.Measure α) (obs : α → β) (P : Set α)
    (hPure : ObservablePureMeasure μ obs P) :
    scanErrorMeasure μ obs P = 0
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  classical
  unfold scanErrorMeasure
  refine (Fintype.sum_eq_zero_iff_of_nonneg (f := fun b =>
    min (cellEventMeasure μ obs P b) (cellComplMeasure μ obs P b)) ?_).2 ?_
  · intro b
    exact bot_le
  · funext b
    rcases hPure b with hEvent | hCompl
    · simp [hEvent]
    · simp [hCompl]

discoveries.lean_statement:
def boundaryCellsMeasure {α β : Type*} [MeasurableSpace α] [Fintype β]
    (μ : MeasureTheory.Measure α) (obs : α → β) (P : Set α) : Finset β
discoveries.lean_proof:
:=
  Finset.univ.filter fun b => cellEventMeasure μ obs P b ≠ 0 ∧ cellComplMeasure μ obs P b ≠ 0
discoveries.docstring: Observation cells where the event is not pure under a general measure.

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem observablePureMeasure_iff_boundaryCellsMeasure_eq_empty
    {α β : Type*} [MeasurableSpace α] [Fintype β]
    (μ : MeasureTheory.Measure α) (obs : α → β) (P : Set α) :
    ObservablePureMeasure μ obs P ↔ boundaryCellsMeasure μ obs P = ∅
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  classical
  constructor
  · intro hPure
    ext b
    constructor
    · intro hb
      have h := hPure b
      simp only [boundaryCellsMeasure, Finset.mem_filter, Finset.mem_univ, true_and] at hb
      rcases h with hEvent | hCompl
      · exact False.elim (hb.1 hEvent)
      · exact False.elim (hb.2 hCompl)
    · intro hb
      cases hb
  · intro hEmpty b
    by_cases hEvent : cellEventMeasure μ obs P b = 0
    · exact Or.inl hEvent
    · right
      by_contra hCompl
      have hb : b ∈ boundaryCellsMeasure μ obs P := by
        simp [boundaryCellsMeasure, hEvent, hCompl]
      have hNotMem : b ∉ boundaryCellsMeasure μ obs P := by
        simp [hEmpty]
      exact hNotMem hb
discoveries.docstring: thm:spg-measure-purity-boundary-empty

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem boundaryCellsMeasure_eq_empty_of_scanErrorMeasure_eq_zero
    {α β : Type*} [MeasurableSpace α] [Fintype β]
    (μ : MeasureTheory.Measure α) (obs : α → β) (P : Set α)
    (hZero : scanErrorMeasure μ obs P = 0) :
    boundaryCellsMeasure μ obs P = ∅
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  classical
  by_contra hNotEmpty
  obtain ⟨b, hb⟩ := Finset.nonempty_iff_ne_empty.mpr hNotEmpty
  have hTerms := (Fintype.sum_eq_zero_iff_of_nonneg (f := fun b =>
    min (cellEventMeasure μ obs P b) (cellComplMeasure μ obs P b)) (by
      intro b
      exact bot_le)).1 (by simpa [scanErrorMeasure] using hZero)
  have hTermZero : min (cellEventMeasure μ obs P b) (cellComplMeasure μ obs P b) = 0 := by
    simpa using congrFun hTerms b
  have hb' : cellEventMeasure μ obs P b ≠ 0 ∧ cellComplMeasure μ obs P b ≠ 0 := by
    simpa [boundaryCellsMeasure] using hb
  have hPosEvent : 0 < cellEventMeasure μ obs P b :=
    pos_iff_ne_zero.mpr hb'.1
  have hPosCompl : 0 < cellComplMeasure μ obs P b :=
    pos_iff_ne_zero.mpr hb'.2
  have hPosMin : 0 < min (cellEventMeasure μ obs P b) (cellComplMeasure μ obs P b) :=
    lt_min hPosEvent hPosCompl
  rw [hTermZero] at hPosMin
  exact lt_irrefl _ hPosMin

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem observablePureMeasure_of_scanErrorMeasure_eq_zero
    {α β : Type*} [MeasurableSpace α] [Fintype β]
    (μ : MeasureTheory.Measure α) (obs : α → β) (P : Set α)
    (hZero : scanErrorMeasure μ obs P = 0) :
    ObservablePureMeasure μ obs P
discoveries.lean_proof:
:=
  (observablePureMeasure_iff_boundaryCellsMeasure_eq_empty μ obs P).2
    (boundaryCellsMeasure_eq_empty_of_scanErrorMeasure_eq_zero μ obs P hZero)

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem scanErrorMeasure_eq_zero_iff_observablePure
    {α β : Type*} [MeasurableSpace α] [Fintype β]
    (μ : MeasureTheory.Measure α) (obs : α → β) (P : Set α) :
    scanErrorMeasure μ obs P = 0 ↔ ObservablePureMeasure μ obs P
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  constructor
  · exact observablePureMeasure_of_scanErrorMeasure_eq_zero μ obs P
  · exact scanErrorMeasure_eq_zero_of_observablePure μ obs P

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem scanErrorMeasure_eq_zero_iff_boundaryCellsMeasure_eq_empty
    {α β : Type*} [MeasurableSpace α] [Fintype β]
    (μ : MeasureTheory.Measure α) (obs : α → β) (P : Set α) :
    scanErrorMeasure μ obs P = 0 ↔ boundaryCellsMeasure μ obs P = ∅
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  rw [scanErrorMeasure_eq_zero_iff_observablePure,
    observablePureMeasure_iff_boundaryCellsMeasure_eq_empty]

@[simp] theorem boundaryCellsMeasure_observableEvent_eq_empty
    {α β : Type*} [MeasurableSpace α] [Fintype β]
    (μ : MeasureTheory.Measure α) (obs : α → β) (A : Set β) :
    boundaryCellsMeasure μ obs (observableEvent obs A) = ∅ := by
  classical
  ext b
  by_cases hA : b ∈ A
  · simp [boundaryCellsMeasure, hA]
  · simp [boundaryCellsMeasure, hA]

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem scanErrorMeasure_eq_sum_boundary {α β : Type*} [MeasurableSpace α] [Fintype β]
    (μ : MeasureTheory.Measure α) (obs : α → β) (P : Set α) :
    scanErrorMeasure μ obs P
      = Finset.sum (boundaryCellsMeasure μ obs P) (fun b =>
          min (cellEventMeasure μ obs P b) (cellComplMeasure μ obs P b))
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  classical
  unfold scanErrorMeasure boundaryCellsMeasure
  rw [← Finset.sum_subset (Finset.subset_univ (boundaryCellsMeasure μ obs P)) (by
    intro b _hb hbNotMem
    simp only [boundaryCellsMeasure, Finset.mem_filter, Finset.mem_univ, true_and] at hbNotMem
    simp only [not_and_or] at hbNotMem
    rcases hbNotMem with hEvent | hCompl
    · have hEvent' : cellEventMeasure μ obs P b = 0 := by simpa using hEvent
      simp [hEvent']
    · have hCompl' : cellComplMeasure μ obs P b = 0 := by simpa using hCompl
      simp [hCompl'])]
  simp [boundaryCellsMeasure]
discoveries.docstring: thm:spg-measure-boundary-decomposition

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem scanErrorMeasure_le_boundaryMass {α β : Type*} [MeasurableSpace α] [Fintype β]
    (μ : MeasureTheory.Measure α) (obs : α → β) (P : Set α) :
    scanErrorMeasure μ obs P
      ≤ Finset.sum (boundaryCellsMeasure μ obs P) (fun b => cellMeasure μ obs b)
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  rw [scanErrorMeasure_eq_sum_boundary]
  refine Finset.sum_le_sum ?_
  intro b _hb
  exact (min_le_left _ _).trans <| by
    unfold cellEventMeasure cellMeasure
    exact MeasureTheory.measure_mono (by intro x hx; exact hx.2)

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem scanErrorMeasure_le_boundaryCard_mul {α β : Type*} [MeasurableSpace α] [Fintype β]
    (μ : MeasureTheory.Measure α) (obs : α → β) (P : Set α) (κ : ENNReal)
    (hκ : ∀ b, cellMeasure μ obs b ≤ κ) :
    scanErrorMeasure μ obs P ≤ (boundaryCellsMeasure μ obs P).card * κ
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  calc
    scanErrorMeasure μ obs P
        ≤ Finset.sum (boundaryCellsMeasure μ obs P) (fun b => cellMeasure μ obs b) :=
          scanErrorMeasure_le_boundaryMass μ obs P
    _ ≤ Finset.sum (boundaryCellsMeasure μ obs P) (fun _b => κ) := by
      refine Finset.sum_le_sum ?_
      intro b hb
      exact hκ b
    _ = (boundaryCellsMeasure μ obs P).card * κ := by
      simp
discoveries.docstring: cor:spg-measure-boundary-upper-bound

    (μ : PMF α) (obs : α → β) (P : Set α) :

discoveries.docstring: thm:boundary-count-pmf-bridge

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem prefixBoundaryCylinderCount_toMeasure_eq {m n : Nat}
    [MeasurableSpace (Word n)] [MeasurableSingletonClass (Word n)]

theorem cellEventMeasure_le_cellMeasure {α β : Type*} [MeasurableSpace α]
    (μ : MeasureTheory.Measure α) (obs : α → β) (P : Set α) (b : β) :
    cellEventMeasure μ obs P b ≤ cellMeasure μ obs b
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  unfold cellEventMeasure cellMeasure
  exact MeasureTheory.measure_mono Set.inter_subset_right
discoveries.docstring:
Cell event mass is bounded by cell total mass (measure monotonicity).
    thm:cell-event-measure-le

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem cellComplMeasure_le_cellMeasure {α β : Type*} [MeasurableSpace α]
    (μ : MeasureTheory.Measure α) (obs : α → β) (P : Set α) (b : β) :
    cellComplMeasure μ obs P b ≤ cellMeasure μ obs b
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  unfold cellComplMeasure cellMeasure
  exact MeasureTheory.measure_mono Set.diff_subset
discoveries.docstring:
Cell complement mass is bounded by cell total mass (measure monotonicity).
    thm:cell-compl-measure-le

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem cellEventMeasure_add_cellComplMeasure_eq_cellMeasure {α β : Type*} [MeasurableSpace α]
    (μ : MeasureTheory.Measure α) (obs : α → β) (P : Set α) (b : β)
    (hP : MeasurableSet P) :
    cellEventMeasure μ obs P b + cellComplMeasure μ obs P b = cellMeasure μ obs b
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  unfold cellEventMeasure cellComplMeasure cellMeasure
  rw [Set.inter_comm P (observableCell obs b)]
  exact MeasureTheory.measure_inter_add_diff _ hP
discoveries.docstring:
Cell partition identity under a measurable event: μ(P ∩ C) + μ(C \ P) = μ(C).
    This is the measure-theoretic analogue of `cellEventMass_add_cellComplMass_eq_cellMass`.
    thm:cell-partition-identity-measure

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem observablePureMeasure_compl {α β : Type*} [MeasurableSpace α] [Fintype β]
    (μ : MeasureTheory.Measure α) (obs : α → β) (P : Set α) :
    ObservablePureMeasure μ obs Pᶜ ↔ ObservablePureMeasure μ obs P
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  constructor <;> intro h b <;> specialize h b
  · rw [cellEventMeasure_compl, cellComplMeasure_compl] at h
    rcases h with h | h
    · exact Or.inr h
    · exact Or.inl h
  · rw [cellEventMeasure_compl, cellComplMeasure_compl]
    rcases h with h | h
    · exact Or.inr h
    · exact Or.inl h
discoveries.docstring:
Observable purity is symmetric under complement of the event (measure).
    thm:observable-pure-compl-measure

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem boundaryCellsMeasure_compl {α β : Type*} [MeasurableSpace α] [Fintype β]
    (μ : MeasureTheory.Measure α) (obs : α → β) (P : Set α) :
    boundaryCellsMeasure μ obs Pᶜ = boundaryCellsMeasure μ obs P
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  ext b
  constructor
  · intro hb
    simp only [boundaryCellsMeasure, Finset.mem_filter, Finset.mem_univ, true_and] at hb ⊢
    rw [cellEventMeasure_compl, cellComplMeasure_compl] at hb
    exact ⟨hb.2, hb.1⟩
  · intro hb
    simp only [boundaryCellsMeasure, Finset.mem_filter, Finset.mem_univ, true_and] at hb ⊢
    rw [cellEventMeasure_compl, cellComplMeasure_compl]
    exact ⟨hb.2, hb.1⟩
discoveries.docstring:
Boundary cells are the same for the event and its complement (measure).
    thm:boundary-cells-compl-measure

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem boundaryCylinderCount_compl {α β : Type*} [MeasurableSpace α] [Fintype β]

discoveries.docstring:
Boundary cylinder count is the same for the event and its complement (measure).

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem prefixBoundaryCellsMeasure_compl [MeasurableSpace (Word n)]

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem observableCell_measurableSet {α β : Type*} [MeasurableSpace α]
    [MeasurableSpace β] [MeasurableSingletonClass β]
    (obs : α → β) (hObs : Measurable obs) (b : β) :
    MeasurableSet (observableCell obs b)
discoveries.docstring: Observable cells are measurable when the observation is measurable.

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem observableCell_pairwiseDisjoint {α β : Type*} [Fintype β] (obs : α → β) :
    Set.PairwiseDisjoint (↑(Finset.univ : Finset β)) (observableCell obs)
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  intro b₁ _ b₂ _ hne
  rw [Function.onFun, Set.disjoint_left]
  intro x hx₁ hx₂
  exact hne (hx₁.symm.trans hx₂)
discoveries.docstring: Observable cells are pairwise disjoint.

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem observableCell_iUnion {α β : Type*} [Fintype β] (obs : α → β) :
    (⋃ b ∈ (Finset.univ : Finset β), observableCell obs b) = Set.univ
discoveries.docstring: The union of all observable cells covers the entire space.

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem cellEventMeasure_sum {α β : Type*} [MeasurableSpace α] [Fintype β]
    [MeasurableSpace β] [MeasurableSingletonClass β]
    (μ : MeasureTheory.Measure α) (obs : α → β) (hObs : Measurable obs)
    (P : Set α) (hP : MeasurableSet P) :
    ∑ b, cellEventMeasure μ obs P b = μ P
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  have hCell := fun b => observableCell_measurableSet obs hObs b
  have hUnion : P = ⋃ b ∈ (Finset.univ : Finset β), P ∩ observableCell obs b := by
    rw [← Set.inter_iUnion₂, observableCell_iUnion, Set.inter_univ]
  have hDisj : Set.PairwiseDisjoint (↑(Finset.univ : Finset β))
      (fun b => P ∩ observableCell obs b) := by
    exact (observableCell_pairwiseDisjoint obs).mono (fun _b => Set.inter_subset_right)
  conv_rhs => rw [hUnion]
  rw [MeasureTheory.measure_biUnion_finset hDisj (fun b _ => hP.inter (hCell b))]
  simp [cellEventMeasure]
discoveries.docstring: Cell event measures sum to the event measure (with measurability hypotheses).

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem cellComplMeasure_sum {α β : Type*} [MeasurableSpace α] [Fintype β]
    [MeasurableSpace β] [MeasurableSingletonClass β]
    (μ : MeasureTheory.Measure α) (obs : α → β) (hObs : Measurable obs)
    (P : Set α) (hP : MeasurableSet P) :
    ∑ b, cellComplMeasure μ obs P b = μ Pᶜ
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  simp_rw [← cellEventMeasure_compl]
  exact cellEventMeasure_sum μ obs hObs Pᶜ hP.compl
discoveries.docstring: Cell complement measures sum to the complement measure (with measurability hypotheses).

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem cellMeasure_sum {α β : Type*} [MeasurableSpace α] [Fintype β]
    [MeasurableSpace β] [MeasurableSingletonClass β]
    (μ : MeasureTheory.Measure α) (obs : α → β) (hObs : Measurable obs) :
    ∑ b, cellMeasure μ obs b = μ Set.univ
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  have : ∀ b, cellMeasure μ obs b = cellEventMeasure μ obs Set.univ b := by
    intro b; simp [cellMeasure, cellEventMeasure, Set.univ_inter]
  simp_rw [this]
  exact cellEventMeasure_sum μ obs hObs Set.univ MeasurableSet.univ
discoveries.docstring: Cell total measures sum to the total measure (with measurability hypotheses).

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem cellEventMeasure_refines_sum_measure {α β γ : Type*}
    [MeasurableSpace α] [Fintype γ] [DecidableEq β]
    [MeasurableSpace γ] [MeasurableSingletonClass γ]
    (μ : MeasureTheory.Measure α) (obs₁ : α → β) (obs₂ : α → γ)
    (f : γ → β) (hRef : ∀ a, obs₁ a = f (obs₂ a))
    (hObs₂ : Measurable obs₂)
    (P : Set α) (hP : MeasurableSet P) (b : β) :
    (Finset.univ.filter (fun c => f c = b)).sum
      (fun c => cellEventMeasure μ obs₂ P c) =
      cellEventMeasure μ obs₁ P b
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  simp only [cellEventMeasure]
  -- P ∩ C_b^{obs₁} = ⋃_{c:f(c)=b} (P ∩ C_c^{obs₂})
  have hUnion : P ∩ observableCell obs₁ b =
      ⋃ c ∈ Finset.univ.filter (fun c => f c = b), P ∩ observableCell obs₂ c := by
    ext x
    simp only [observableCell, Set.mem_inter_iff, Set.mem_setOf_eq,
      Set.mem_iUnion, Finset.mem_filter, Finset.mem_univ, true_and]
    constructor
    · intro ⟨hxP, hxObs⟩
      exact ⟨obs₂ x, ⟨by rw [← hRef x, hxObs], hxP, rfl⟩⟩
    · intro ⟨c, hfc, hxP, hxObs₂⟩
      exact ⟨hxP, by rw [hRef x, hxObs₂, hfc]⟩
  rw [hUnion]
  -- The pieces are pairwise disjoint and measurable
  have hDisj : Set.PairwiseDisjoint
      (↑(Finset.univ.filter (fun c => f c = b) : Finset γ))
      (fun c => P ∩ observableCell obs₂ c) := by
    exact ((observableCell_pairwiseDisjoint obs₂).subset
      (by simp [Finset.coe_filter, Set.subset_univ])).mono
        (fun _c => Set.inter_subset_right)
  have hMeas : ∀ c ∈ Finset.univ.filter (fun c => f c = b),
      MeasurableSet (P ∩ observableCell obs₂ c) := by
    intro c _; exact hP.inter (observableCell_measurableSet obs₂ hObs₂ c)
  exact (MeasureTheory.measure_biUnion_finset hDisj hMeas).symm
discoveries.docstring:
Cell event measure decomposes along a refined observation (measure version).
    If obs₁ = f ∘ obs₂ and obs₂ is measurable, then
    ∑_{c : f(c)=b} μ(P ∩ C_c) = μ(P ∩ C_b).

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem scanErrorMeasure_antitone_of_refines {α β γ : Type*}
    [MeasurableSpace α] [Fintype β] [Fintype γ]
    [MeasurableSpace γ] [MeasurableSingletonClass γ] [DecidableEq β]
    (μ : MeasureTheory.Measure α) (obs₁ : α → β) (obs₂ : α → γ)
    (f : γ → β) (hRef : ∀ x, obs₁ x = f (obs₂ x))
    (hObs₂ : Measurable obs₂) (P : Set α) (hP : MeasurableSet P) :
    scanErrorMeasure μ obs₂ P ≤ scanErrorMeasure μ obs₁ P
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  classical
  let filt := fun b => Finset.univ.filter (fun c => f c = b)
  let g := fun c => min (cellEventMeasure μ obs₂ P c) (cellComplMeasure μ obs₂ P c)
  suffices hKey : ∀ b : β,
      (filt b).sum g ≤ min (cellEventMeasure μ obs₁ P b) (cellComplMeasure μ obs₁ P b) by
    show (∑ c, g c) ≤ ∑ b, min (cellEventMeasure μ obs₁ P b) (cellComplMeasure μ obs₁ P b)
    have hFib : ∑ c, g c = ∑ b, (filt b).sum g := by
      symm
      rw [← Finset.sum_biUnion]
      · refine Finset.sum_congr ?_ (fun _ _ => rfl)
        ext c; simp [filt, Finset.mem_biUnion, Finset.mem_filter]
      · intro b₁ _ b₂ _ hne
        exact Finset.disjoint_filter.2 (fun c _ h₁ h₂ => hne (h₁.symm.trans h₂))
    rw [hFib]
    exact Finset.sum_le_sum (fun b _ => hKey b)
  intro b
  calc (filt b).sum g
      ≤ min ((filt b).sum (fun c => cellEventMeasure μ obs₂ P c))
          ((filt b).sum (fun c => cellComplMeasure μ obs₂ P c)) :=
        le_min (Finset.sum_le_sum (fun i _ => min_le_left _ _))
          (Finset.sum_le_sum (fun i _ => min_le_right _ _))
    _ = min (cellEventMeasure μ obs₁ P b) (cellComplMeasure μ obs₁ P b) := by
        rw [cellEventMeasure_refines_sum_measure μ obs₁ obs₂ f hRef hObs₂ P hP b]
        simp_rw [← cellEventMeasure_compl]
        rw [cellEventMeasure_refines_sum_measure μ obs₁ obs₂ f hRef hObs₂ Pᶜ hP.compl b,
          cellEventMeasure_compl]
discoveries.docstring: Observation refinement reduces scan error (direct measure version with measurability).

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem prefixScanErrorMeasure_antitone_direct {m₁ m₂ n : Nat}
    [MeasurableSpace (Word n)]
    [MeasurableSpace (Word m₂)] [MeasurableSingletonClass (Word m₂)]
    (μ : MeasureTheory.Measure (Word n))
    (h₁ : m₁ ≤ n) (h₂ : m₂ ≤ n) (hm : m₁ ≤ m₂)
    (hObs : Measurable (prefixObservation h₂))
    (P : Set (Word n)) (hP : MeasurableSet P) :
    prefixScanErrorMeasure μ h₂ P ≤ prefixScanErrorMeasure μ h₁ P
discoveries.lean_proof:
:=
  scanErrorMeasure_antitone_of_refines μ (prefixObservation h₁) (prefixObservation h₂)
    (restrictWord hm) (fun w => (restrictWord_comp hm h₂ w).symm) hObs P hP
discoveries.docstring:
Prefix scan error is monotonically non-increasing in resolution (direct measure version).
    This requires measurability of the prefix observation.

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem scanErrorMeasure_le_half {α β : Type*} [MeasurableSpace α] [Fintype β]
    [MeasurableSpace β] [MeasurableSingletonClass β]
    (μ : MeasureTheory.Measure α) [MeasureTheory.IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
    (obs : α → β) (hObs : Measurable obs) (P : Set α) (hP : MeasurableSet P) :
    2 * scanErrorMeasure μ obs P ≤ 1
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  have hBound := scanErrorMeasure_le_min μ obs P
  have hEventSum := cellEventMeasure_sum μ obs hObs P hP
  have hComplSum := cellComplMeasure_sum μ obs hObs P hP
  calc 2 * scanErrorMeasure μ obs P
      ≤ 2 * min (∑ b, cellEventMeasure μ obs P b) (∑ b, cellComplMeasure μ obs P b) := by
        exact mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left hBound (by norm_num)
    _ = min (∑ b, cellEventMeasure μ obs P b) (∑ b, cellComplMeasure μ obs P b) +
        min (∑ b, cellEventMeasure μ obs P b) (∑ b, cellComplMeasure μ obs P b) := two_mul _
    _ ≤ (∑ b, cellEventMeasure μ obs P b) + (∑ b, cellComplMeasure μ obs P b) :=
        add_le_add (min_le_left _ _) (min_le_right _ _)
    _ = μ P + μ Pᶜ := by rw [hEventSum, hComplSum]
    _ = μ Set.univ := by
        rw [← MeasureTheory.measure_inter_add_diff Set.univ hP]
        simp [Set.univ_inter, Set.diff_eq, Set.univ_inter]
    _ = 1 := MeasureTheory.IsProbabilityMeasure.measure_univ
discoveries.docstring: Scan error under a probability measure is bounded by 1/2 (with measurability).

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem cellEventMeasure_mono {α β : Type*} [MeasurableSpace α]
    (μ : MeasureTheory.Measure α) (obs : α → β) {P Q : Set α} (h : P ⊆ Q) (b : β) :
    cellEventMeasure μ obs P b ≤ cellEventMeasure μ obs Q b
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  unfold cellEventMeasure
  exact MeasureTheory.measure_mono (Set.inter_subset_inter_left _ h)
discoveries.docstring: Scan error is monotone in cell event mass: if P ⊆ Q then cellEventMeasure(P) ≤ cellEventMeasure(Q).

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem scanErrorMeasure_at_empty {α β : Type*} [MeasurableSpace α] [Fintype β]

    this is a named alias for the Frontier layer.

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem scanErrorMeasure_at_univ {α β : Type*} [MeasurableSpace α] [Fintype β]

    scanErrorMeasure μ obs Set.univ = 0
discoveries.docstring: Scan error at the full event is zero (measure).

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem cellComplMeasure_anti {α β : Type*} [MeasurableSpace α]
    (μ : MeasureTheory.Measure α) (obs : α → β) {P Q : Set α} (h : P ⊆ Q) (b : β) :

discoveries.docstring: Cell complement measure is monotone in the reverse direction of event containment.

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem scanErrorMeasure_of_univ_eq_zero {α β : Type*} [MeasurableSpace α] [Fintype β]
    (μ : MeasureTheory.Measure α) (obs : α → β) :
    scanErrorMeasure μ obs Set.univ = 0

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem scanErrorMeasure_le_cellEventSum {α β : Type*} [MeasurableSpace α] [Fintype β]
    (μ : MeasureTheory.Measure α) (obs : α → β) (P : Set α) :
    scanErrorMeasure μ obs P ≤ ∑ b, cellEventMeasure μ obs P b

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem scanErrorMeasure_le_cellComplSum {α β : Type*} [MeasurableSpace α] [Fintype β]
    (μ : MeasureTheory.Measure α) (obs : α → β) (P : Set α) :
    scanErrorMeasure μ obs P ≤ ∑ b, cellComplMeasure μ obs P b

discoveries.lean_statement:

discoveries.lean_statement:

discoveries.lean_statement:

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem observablePureMeasure_of_observable {α β : Type*} [MeasurableSpace α] [Fintype β]

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem scanErrorMeasure_complement {α β : Type*} [MeasurableSpace α] [Fintype β]

    scanErrorMeasure μ obs Pᶜ = scanErrorMeasure μ obs P

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem boundaryCellsMeasure_complement {α β : Type*} [MeasurableSpace α] [Fintype β]
    (μ : MeasureTheory.Measure α) (obs : α → β) (P : Set α) :
    boundaryCellsMeasure μ obs Pᶜ = boundaryCellsMeasure μ obs P

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem cell_partition_measure {α β : Type*} [MeasurableSpace α]
    (μ : MeasureTheory.Measure α) (obs : α → β) (P : Set α) (b : β)
    (hP : MeasurableSet P) :
    cellEventMeasure μ obs P b + cellComplMeasure μ obs P b = cellMeasure μ obs b

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem scanErrorMeasure_bayes_general {α β : Type*} [MeasurableSpace α] [Fintype β]
    (μ : MeasureTheory.Measure α) (obs : α → β) (P : Set α) :
    scanErrorMeasure μ obs P ≤ min
        (∑ b, cellEventMeasure μ obs P b) (∑ b, cellComplMeasure μ obs P b)
discoveries.docstring:
Scan error under a general finite measure is bounded by min of event and complement
    cell sums (Bayes bound for general measures, Plan 17/18).

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem scanErrorMeasure_le_event_measure {α β : Type*} [MeasurableSpace α] [Fintype β]
    [MeasurableSpace β] [MeasurableSingletonClass β]
    (μ : MeasureTheory.Measure α) (obs : α → β) (hObs : Measurable obs)
    (P : Set α) (hP : MeasurableSet P) :
    scanErrorMeasure μ obs P ≤ μ P
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  calc scanErrorMeasure μ obs P
      ≤ ∑ b, cellEventMeasure μ obs P b := scanErrorMeasure_le_cellEventSum μ obs P
    _ = μ P := cellEventMeasure_sum μ obs hObs P hP
discoveries.docstring: Scan error is bounded by the event measure (with measurability).

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem scanErrorMeasure_le_compl_measure {α β : Type*} [MeasurableSpace α] [Fintype β]
    [MeasurableSpace β] [MeasurableSingletonClass β]
    (μ : MeasureTheory.Measure α) (obs : α → β) (hObs : Measurable obs)
    (P : Set α) (hP : MeasurableSet P) :
    scanErrorMeasure μ obs P ≤ μ Pᶜ
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  calc scanErrorMeasure μ obs P
      ≤ ∑ b, cellComplMeasure μ obs P b := scanErrorMeasure_le_cellComplSum μ obs P
    _ = μ Pᶜ := cellComplMeasure_sum μ obs hObs P hP
discoveries.docstring: Scan error is bounded by the complement measure (with measurability).

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem scanErrorMeasure_le_min_event_compl {α β : Type*} [MeasurableSpace α] [Fintype β]
    [MeasurableSpace β] [MeasurableSingletonClass β]
    (μ : MeasureTheory.Measure α) (obs : α → β) (hObs : Measurable obs)
    (P : Set α) (hP : MeasurableSet P) :
    scanErrorMeasure μ obs P ≤ min (μ P) (μ Pᶜ)
discoveries.lean_proof:
:=
  le_min (scanErrorMeasure_le_event_measure μ obs hObs P hP)
    (scanErrorMeasure_le_compl_measure μ obs hObs P hP)
discoveries.docstring: Scan error bounded by min of event and complement measures (with measurability).

discoveries.lean_statement:

discoveries.docstring: Scan error is non-negative (trivially for ENNReal).

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem boundaryCylinderCount_observable_zero {α β : Type*} [MeasurableSpace α] [Fintype β]
    (μ : MeasureTheory.Measure α) (obs : α → β) (A : Set β) :

-- ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
-- Phase R150: Measure-theoretic scan error intersection bound
-- ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

private theorem scanErrorMeasure_union_le {α β : Type*} [MeasurableSpace α] [Fintype β]
    (μ : MeasureTheory.Measure α) (obs : α → β) (P Q : Set α) :
    scanErrorMeasure μ obs (P ∪ Q) ≤ scanErrorMeasure μ obs P + scanErrorMeasure μ obs Q
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  unfold scanErrorMeasure
  rw [← Finset.sum_add_distrib]
  refine Finset.sum_le_sum (fun b _ => ?_)
  -- min(μ((P∪Q)∩C), μ(C\(P∪Q))) ≤ min(μ(P∩C), μ(C\P)) + min(μ(Q∩C), μ(C\Q))
  have hev : cellEventMeasure μ obs (P ∪ Q) b ≤
      cellEventMeasure μ obs P b + cellEventMeasure μ obs Q b := by
    simp only [cellEventMeasure]
    calc μ ((P ∪ Q) ∩ observableCell obs b)
        ≤ μ (P ∩ observableCell obs b ∪ Q ∩ observableCell obs b) := by
          apply MeasureTheory.measure_mono; intro x ⟨hpq, hc⟩
          rcases hpq with hp | hq
          · exact Or.inl ⟨hp, hc⟩
          · exact Or.inr ⟨hq, hc⟩
      _ ≤ μ (P ∩ observableCell obs b) + μ (Q ∩ observableCell obs b) :=
          MeasureTheory.measure_union_le _ _
  have hcl : cellComplMeasure μ obs (P ∪ Q) b ≤ cellComplMeasure μ obs P b := by
    apply MeasureTheory.measure_mono
    intro x hx; exact ⟨hx.1, fun hp => hx.2 (Or.inl hp)⟩
  have hcr : cellComplMeasure μ obs (P ∪ Q) b ≤ cellComplMeasure μ obs Q b := by
    apply MeasureTheory.measure_mono
    intro x hx; exact ⟨hx.1, fun hq => hx.2 (Or.inr hq)⟩
  -- min(a,b) ≤ min(a₁,b₁) + min(a₂,b₂) from a ≤ a₁+a₂, b ≤ b₁, b ≤ b₂
  rcases le_total (cellEventMeasure μ obs P b) (cellComplMeasure μ obs P b) with h₁ | h₁
  · rcases le_total (cellEventMeasure μ obs Q b) (cellComplMeasure μ obs Q b) with h₂ | h₂
    · calc min (cellEventMeasure μ obs (P ∪ Q) b) (cellComplMeasure μ obs (P ∪ Q) b)
          ≤ cellEventMeasure μ obs (P ∪ Q) b := min_le_left _ _
        _ ≤ cellEventMeasure μ obs P b + cellEventMeasure μ obs Q b := hev
        _ = min _ _ + min _ _ := by rw [min_eq_left h₁, min_eq_left h₂]
    · calc min _ _ ≤ cellComplMeasure μ obs (P ∪ Q) b := min_le_right _ _
        _ ≤ cellComplMeasure μ obs Q b := hcr
        _ ≤ _ + _ := le_add_left (le_refl _)
        _ = min _ _ + min _ _ := by rw [min_eq_left h₁, min_eq_right h₂]
  · calc min _ _ ≤ cellComplMeasure μ obs (P ∪ Q) b := min_le_right _ _
      _ ≤ cellComplMeasure μ obs P b := hcl
      _ ≤ _ + _ := le_add_right (le_refl _)
      _ = min _ _ + min _ _ := by rw [min_eq_right h₁]
discoveries.docstring: PMF bridge for purity.

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem scanErrorMeasure_inter_le {α β : Type*} [MeasurableSpace α] [Fintype β]
    (μ : MeasureTheory.Measure α) (obs : α → β) (P Q : Set α) :
    scanErrorMeasure μ obs (P ∩ Q) ≤ scanErrorMeasure μ obs P + scanErrorMeasure μ obs Q
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  rw [show P ∩ Q = (Pᶜ ∪ Qᶜ)ᶜ from by ext x; simp []]
  rw [scanErrorMeasure_compl]
  calc scanErrorMeasure μ obs (Pᶜ ∪ Qᶜ)
      ≤ scanErrorMeasure μ obs Pᶜ + scanErrorMeasure μ obs Qᶜ :=
        scanErrorMeasure_union_le μ obs Pᶜ Qᶜ
    _ = scanErrorMeasure μ obs P + scanErrorMeasure μ obs Q := by
        rw [scanErrorMeasure_compl μ obs P, scanErrorMeasure_compl μ obs Q]

end

end Omega.SPG

discoveries.lean_statement: def cyclicPerm (n : ℕ) : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ℤ
discoveries.lean_proof:
:=
  fun i j => if j = Fin.cycle i then 1 else 0
  where
  Fin.cycle (i : Fin n) : Fin n := ⟨(i.val + 1) % n, Nat.mod_lt _ (Fin.pos i)⟩
discoveries.docstring:
The n×n cyclic permutation matrix over ℤ: (Π_n)_{i,j} = 1 if j ≡ i+1 (mod n), else 0.
    prop:cycle-permutation-determinant

/-! ## Trace of cyclic permutation powers

The trace Tr(Π_n^k) = n if n|k, else 0.
This is the key input for the Witt/Möbius primitive counting.
-/
discoveries.docstring: Π_6⁶ = I (cyclic permutation of order 6).

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem cyclicPerm2_trace_powers :
    cyclicPerm2.trace = 0 ∧ (cyclicPerm2 ^ 2).trace = 2 ∧
    (cyclicPerm2 ^ 3).trace = 0 ∧ (cyclicPerm2 ^ 4).trace = 2
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  refine ⟨by native_decide, by native_decide, by native_decide, by native_decide⟩
discoveries.docstring:
Trace of Π_2^k: equals 2 when k is even, 0 when k is odd.
    subsec:operator-zeta-interface

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem cyclicPerm4_fredholm_det (t : ℤ) :
    (1 - t • cyclicPerm4).det = 1 - t ^ 4
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  have h : (1 : Matrix (Fin 4) (Fin 4) ℤ) - t • cyclicPerm4 =
    !![1, -t, 0, 0; 0, 1, -t, 0; 0, 0, 1, -t; -t, 0, 0, 1] := by
    ext i j; fin_cases i <;> fin_cases j <;> simp [cyclicPerm4]
  rw [h, det_four_of]; ring

det(I - t·Π_4) = 1 - t⁴ for the 4×4 cyclic permutation matrix.

    ext i j; fin_cases i <;> fin_cases j <;> simp [cyclicPerm5]
  rw [h, det_five_of]; ring

set_option maxHeartbeats 400000 in
discoveries.docstring:
det(I - t·Π_5) = 1 - t⁵ for the 5×5 cyclic permutation matrix.

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem cyclicPerm6_fredholm_det (t : ℤ) :
    (1 - t • cyclicPerm6).det = 1 - t ^ 6
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  have h : (1 : Matrix (Fin 6) (Fin 6) ℤ) - t • cyclicPerm6 =
    !![1, -t, 0, 0, 0, 0; 0, 1, -t, 0, 0, 0; 0, 0, 1, -t, 0, 0;
       0, 0, 0, 1, -t, 0; 0, 0, 0, 0, 1, -t; -t, 0, 0, 0, 0, 1] := by
    ext i j; fin_cases i <;> fin_cases j <;> simp [cyclicPerm6]
  rw [h, det_six_of]
  -- Now we have 6 terms of 5×5 determinants, most with zero coefficients
  simp only [det_five_of]
  ring

/-! ## Euler product structure

For the cyclic block C(n,α) = α·Π_n, det(I - r·C(n,α)) = 1 - (αr)^n.
This gives the Euler product factorization of finite-state zeta functions.
cor:cyclic-euler-product -/
discoveries.docstring:
det(I - t·Π_6) = 1 - t⁶ for the 6×6 cyclic permutation matrix.
    prop:cycle-permutation-determinant

/-! ## Resolvent trace jump index

The number of poles of det(I-zT)^{-1} inside the unit disk equals
the trace class rank (finite case). For Π_n, all n eigenvalues lie
on the unit circle.

thm:operator-resolvent-trace-jump-index -/
discoveries.docstring:
Euler factor for n=6: det(I - r·(α·Π_6)) = 1 - (αr)⁶.
    cor:cyclic-euler-product

/-! ## 2π i periodicity certificate

Finite-state zeta functions have a strict 2πi/log(λ_max) periodicity,
incompatible with the Riemann zeta's non-periodic structure on the critical line.
We verify the periodicity order: Π_n^n = I.

thm:operator-finite-state-zeta-2pii-periodic-separation -/
discoveries.docstring:
For Π_6: exactly 6 eigenvalues on the unit circle.
    Trace(Π_6^0) = 6 = dim.

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem cyclic_periodicity_orders :
    cyclicPerm2 ^ 2 = 1 ∧ cyclicPerm3 ^ 3 = 1 ∧
    cyclicPerm4 ^ 4 = 1 ∧ cyclicPerm5 ^ 5 = 1 ∧ cyclicPerm6 ^ 6 = 1
discoveries.docstring:
Periodicity orders of cyclic permutation matrices.
    thm:operator-finite-state-zeta-2pii-periodic-separation

    (1 - t ^ 2) * (1 - t ^ 3) = 1 - t ^ 2 - t ^ 3 + t ^ 5

discoveries.lean_statement: def fredholmGoldenMean (z : ℤ) : Matrix (Fin 2) (Fin 2) ℤ
discoveries.docstring:
The "Fredholm matrix" I - z·A for the golden-mean adjacency,
    computed as a matrix over ℤ[z] (here we specialize to concrete z : ℤ).
    def:fredholm-determinant

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem fredholmGoldenMean_det (z : ℤ) :
    (fredholmGoldenMean z).det = 1 - z - z ^ 2
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  simp only [fredholmGoldenMean, Graph.goldenMeanAdjacency]
  simp [det_fin_two]
  ring
discoveries.docstring:
det(I - z·A) = 1 - z - z² for the golden-mean adjacency matrix.
    This is the denominator of ζ_A(z) = det(I - zA)⁻¹.
    subsec:operator-zeta-interface, def:fredholm-determinant

discoveries.lean_statement: theorem fredholmGoldenMean_at_one : (fredholmGoldenMean 1).det = -1

/-! ## Trace sequence for golden-mean matrix

The trace sequence Tr(A^n) satisfies the Fibonacci-like recurrence
inherited from the characteristic polynomial z² - z - 1.
We give concrete values and verify the recurrence.
-/
discoveries.docstring:
Fredholm determinant at z=0: det(I - 0·A) = 1 (normalization).
    subsec:operator-zeta-interface

discoveries.lean_statement:

theorem goldenMean_trace_recurrence :
    ∀ n, n ≤ 6 →
      (Graph.goldenMeanAdjacency ^ (n + 2)).trace =
        (Graph.goldenMeanAdjacency ^ (n + 1)).trace +
        (Graph.goldenMeanAdjacency ^ n).trace

  intro n hn; interval_cases n <;> native_decide

/-! ## Primitive orbit counts via Möbius inversion

For the golden-mean SFT, the number of primitive periodic orbits of length n
is p(n) = (1/n) Σ_{d|n} μ(d) · Tr(A^{n/d}).
We verify p(1) = 1, p(2) = 1, p(3) = 1, p(4) = 1, p(5) = 2, p(6) = 2.
These are the "necklace counts" for the golden-mean constraint.

prop:zetaK-mobius-primitive -/
discoveries.docstring:
The trace sequence Tr(A^n) = L(n) (Lucas numbers) satisfies the recurrence
    Tr(A^{n+2}) = Tr(A^{n+1}) + Tr(A^n), verified for n = 0..6.
    thm:zeta-syntax-trace-linear-recurrence

Primitive orbit count certificate: n · p(n) = Σ_{d|n} μ(d) · a(n/d).
    We verify the numerator n·p(n) directly for n = 1..6.
    prop:zetaK-mobius-primitive

Primitive orbit counts for golden-mean SFT via Mobius inversion, n=7..10.
    p(7)=4, p(8)=5, p(9)=8, p(10)=11.
    n·p(n) = Σ_{d|n} μ(n/d) · L(d).
    prop:zetaK-mobius-primitive

discoveries.lean_proof:
:=
  goldenMean_primitive_orbit_7_10

/-! ## Degeneracy-zeta coefficients

The degeneracy ratio ζ_full/ζ = (1-z-z²)/(1-2z) measures the gap
between the full 2-shift and the golden-mean SFT.
We verify: 2^n - Tr(A^n) for n = 1..6.
rem:degeneracy-zeta-bridge -/
discoveries.docstring: Paper: prop:zetaK-mobius-primitive (n=7..10)

discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by omega

/-! ## Characteristic polynomial identity

The golden-mean adjacency matrix satisfies χ_A(λ) = λ² - λ - 1.
This is the key input for the trace recurrence (Cayley-Hamilton).
-/
discoveries.docstring:
Degeneracy ghost coefficients: 2^n - L(n) for n = 1..8.
    rem:degeneracy-zeta-bridge

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem goldenMean_cayleyHamilton :
    Graph.goldenMeanAdjacency ^ 2 - Graph.goldenMeanAdjacency - 1 = 0
discoveries.docstring:
Characteristic polynomial check: A² - A - I = 0 (Cayley-Hamilton for golden-mean).
    thm:zeta-syntax-trace-linear-recurrence

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem goldenMean_trace_recurrence_unbounded (n : ℕ) :
    (Graph.goldenMeanAdjacency ^ (n + 2)).trace =
      (Graph.goldenMeanAdjacency ^ (n + 1)).trace +
      (Graph.goldenMeanAdjacency ^ n).trace
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  -- Cayley-Hamilton gives A² = A + 1
  have hCH : Graph.goldenMeanAdjacency ^ 2 = Graph.goldenMeanAdjacency + 1 := by
    native_decide
  -- A^(n+2) = A^n * A² = A^n * (A + 1) = A^(n+1) + A^n
  have hpow : Graph.goldenMeanAdjacency ^ (n + 2) =
      Graph.goldenMeanAdjacency ^ (n + 1) + Graph.goldenMeanAdjacency ^ n := by
    rw [pow_add, hCH, mul_add, mul_one, ← pow_succ]
  rw [hpow, Matrix.trace_add]
discoveries.docstring:
The trace recurrence Tr(A^{n+2}) = Tr(A^{n+1}) + Tr(A^n) holds for ALL n,
    not just n ≤ 6. Proved algebraically via the Cayley-Hamilton theorem
    for the 2×2 golden-mean adjacency matrix with char poly z² - z - 1.
    thm:zeta-syntax-trace-linear-recurrence

discoveries.lean_statement:
def lucasNum : ℕ → ℤ
  | 0 => 2
  | 1 => 1
  | n + 2 => lucasNum (n + 1) + lucasNum n

@[simp] theorem lucasNum_zero : lucasNum 0 = 2
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= rfl
@[simp] theorem lucasNum_one : lucasNum 1 = 1 := rfl
@[simp] theorem lucasNum_succ_succ (n : ℕ) :
    lucasNum (n + 2) = lucasNum (n + 1) + lucasNum n := rfl
discoveries.docstring:
Lucas numbers: L(0)=2, L(1)=1, L(n+2)=L(n+1)+L(n).
    thm:zeta-syntax-trace-linear-recurrence

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem trace_eq_lucasNum (n : ℕ) :
    (Graph.goldenMeanAdjacency ^ n).trace = lucasNum n
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  induction n using Nat.strongRecOn with
  | _ n ih =>
    match n with
    | 0 => native_decide
    | 1 => native_decide
    | n + 2 =>
      rw [goldenMean_trace_recurrence_unbounded n, ih (n + 1) (by omega),
        ih n (by omega), lucasNum_succ_succ]
discoveries.docstring:
The trace of A^n equals the n-th Lucas number L(n).
    thm:zeta-syntax-trace-linear-recurrence

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem lucasNum_eq_fib_sum (n : ℕ) :
    lucasNum (n + 2) = (Nat.fib (n + 1) : ℤ) + Nat.fib (n + 3)
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  induction n using Nat.strongRecOn with
  | _ n ih =>
    match n with
    | 0 => norm_num [lucasNum, Nat.fib]
    | 1 => norm_num [lucasNum, Nat.fib]
    | n + 2 =>
      rw [lucasNum_succ_succ (n + 2)]
      rw [ih (n + 1) (by omega), ih n (by omega)]
      rw [show n + 1 + 3 = n + 4 from by omega,
          show n + 2 + 1 = n + 3 from by omega,
          show n + 2 + 3 = n + 5 from by omega]
      have hf3 : (Nat.fib (n + 3) : ℤ) = Nat.fib (n + 2) + Nat.fib (n + 1) := by
        push_cast [Nat.fib_add_two]; ring
      have hf5 : (Nat.fib (n + 5) : ℤ) = Nat.fib (n + 4) + Nat.fib (n + 3) := by
        push_cast [Nat.fib_add_two]; ring
      linarith
discoveries.docstring:
Lucas-Fibonacci relation: L(n+2) = F(n+1) + F(n+3).
    thm:zeta-syntax-trace-linear-recurrence

discoveries.lean_statement: theorem lucasNum_pos (n : ℕ) : 0 < lucasNum n
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  induction n using Nat.strongRecOn with
  | _ n ih =>
    match n with
    | 0 => simp [lucasNum]
    | 1 => simp [lucasNum]
    | n + 2 =>
      rw [lucasNum_succ_succ]
      linarith [ih (n + 1) (by omega), ih n (by omega)]
discoveries.docstring:
Lucas numbers are strictly positive.
    thm:zeta-syntax-trace-linear-recurrence

discoveries.lean_statement: theorem degeneracy_ghost_positive (n : ℕ) (hn : 3 ≤ n) : 0 < (2 : ℤ) ^ n - lucasNum n
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  induction n using Nat.strongRecOn with
  | _ n ih =>
    match n, hn with
    | 3, _ => simp [lucasNum]
    | 4, _ => simp [lucasNum]
    | n + 5, _ =>
      rw [lucasNum_succ_succ]
      have h1 := ih (n + 4) (by omega) (by omega)
      have h2 := ih (n + 3) (by omega) (by omega)
      have hp1 : (2 : ℤ) ^ (n + 5) = 2 * 2 ^ (n + 4) := by ring
      have hp2 : (2 : ℤ) ^ (n + 4) = 2 * 2 ^ (n + 3) := by ring
      have hp3 : (0 : ℤ) < 2 ^ (n + 3) := by positivity
      linarith
discoveries.docstring:
The degeneracy ghost 2^n - L(n) is strictly positive for n ≥ 3,
    proved by strong induction using 2^(n+2) - L(n+2) = 2·(2^(n+1) - L(n+1)) - (2^n - L(n)).
    rem:degeneracy-zeta-bridge

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem goldenMean_trace_eq_fib_sum (n : ℕ) :
    (Graph.goldenMeanAdjacency ^ (n + 2)).trace =
      (Nat.fib (n + 1) : ℤ) + Nat.fib (n + 3)
discoveries.docstring:
Trace of A^(n+2) equals F(n+1) + F(n+3), combining trace=Lucas and Lucas=Fib sum.
    thm:zeta-syntax-trace-linear-recurrence

The degeneracy ghost sequence d_n = 2^n - L(n) satisfies
    d_{n+3} = 3·d_{n+2} - d_{n+1} - 2·d_n, verified for n=1..6.
    d_1=1, d_2=1, d_3=4, d_4=9, d_5=21, d_6=46, d_7=99, d_8=209, d_9=436.
    rem:degeneracy-zeta-bridge
Paper: rem:degeneracy-zeta-bridge (ghost recurrence)

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem degeneracy_ghost_recurrence_general (n : ℕ) :
    (2 : ℤ) ^ (n + 3) - lucasNum (n + 3) =
      3 * ((2 : ℤ) ^ (n + 2) - lucasNum (n + 2)) -
      ((2 : ℤ) ^ (n + 1) - lucasNum (n + 1)) -
      2 * ((2 : ℤ) ^ n - lucasNum n)
discoveries.docstring:
The degeneracy ghost d_n = 2^n - L(n) satisfies
    d_{n+3} = 3·d_{n+2} - d_{n+1} - 2·d_n for all n.
    rem:degeneracy-zeta-bridge

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem lucasNum_mod2_period_three (n : Nat) :
    lucasNum (n + 3) % 2 = lucasNum n % 2
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  -- L(n+3) = L(n+2) + L(n+1) = (L(n+1) + L(n)) + L(n+1) = 2·L(n+1) + L(n)
  rw [lucasNum_succ_succ (n + 1), lucasNum_succ_succ n]
  omega
discoveries.docstring:
Lucas numbers mod 2 have period 3: L(n+3) ≡ L(n) (mod 2) for all n.
    rem:degeneracy-zeta-bridge

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem degeneracy_ghost_strict_mono (n : ℕ) (hn : 3 ≤ n) :
    (2 : ℤ) ^ n - lucasNum n < (2 : ℤ) ^ (n + 1) - lucasNum (n + 1)
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  -- d_{n+1} - d_n = 2^n - L(n-1) > 0 for n ≥ 3
  -- Suffices: L(n-1) < 2^n
  match n, hn with
  | 3, _ => simp [lucasNum]
  | n + 4, _ =>
    -- L(n+4+1) = L(n+4) + L(n+3), so need 2^(n+4) - L(n+4) < 2^(n+5) - L(n+5)
    -- L(n+5) = L(n+4) + L(n+3)
    rw [show n + 4 + 1 = (n + 3) + 2 from by omega, lucasNum_succ_succ (n + 3)]
    -- Need: L(n+3) < 2^(n+4)
    have hpos := degeneracy_ghost_positive (n + 3) (by omega)
    -- hpos: 0 < 2^(n+3) - L(n+3), so L(n+3) < 2^(n+3) ≤ 2^(n+4)
    have hp1 : (2 : ℤ) ^ (n + 3 + 2) = 4 * 2 ^ (n + 3) := by ring
    have hp2 : (2 : ℤ) ^ (n + 4) = 2 * 2 ^ (n + 3) := by ring
    -- Normalize n + 3 + 1 to n + 4; then linarith with power-of-2 identities
    have hn34 : lucasNum (n + 3 + 1) = lucasNum (n + 4) := by congr 1
    simp only [hn34, hp1, hp2]
    have hpow_pos : (0 : ℤ) < 2 ^ (n + 3) := by positivity
    linarith
discoveries.docstring:
The degeneracy ghost d_n = 2^n - L(n) is strictly increasing for n ≥ 3.
    rem:degeneracy-zeta-bridge

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem degeneracy_ghost_doubling (n : Nat) (hn : 4 ≤ n) :
    2 * ((2 : ℤ) ^ n - lucasNum n) ≤ (2 : ℤ) ^ (n + 1) - lucasNum (n + 1)
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  -- 2*d_n ≤ d_{n+1} iff L(n+1) ≤ 2*L(n) iff L(n-1) ≤ L(n)
  -- Expand L(n+1) = L(n) + L(n-1), then 2^{n+1} = 2*2^n reduces goal to L(n-1) ≤ L(n)
  obtain ⟨k, rfl⟩ := Nat.exists_eq_add_of_le hn
  -- n = k + 4. Goal: 2*(2^(k+4) - L(k+4)) ≤ 2^(k+5) - L(k+5)
  have hL : lucasNum (k + 5) = lucasNum (k + 4) + lucasNum (k + 3) := by
    have := lucasNum_succ_succ (k + 3)
    simp only [show k + 3 + 2 = k + 5 from by omega, show k + 3 + 1 = k + 4 from by omega] at this
    exact this
  have hpow : (2 : ℤ) ^ (k + 5) = 2 * (2 : ℤ) ^ (k + 4) := by
    rw [show k + 5 = (k + 4) + 1 from by omega, pow_succ]; ring
  have hLmono : lucasNum (k + 3) ≤ lucasNum (k + 4) := by
    have := lucasNum_succ_succ (k + 2)
    simp only [show k + 2 + 2 = k + 4 from by omega, show k + 2 + 1 = k + 3 from by omega] at this
    linarith [lucasNum_pos (k + 2)]
  rw [show (4 : ℕ) + k = k + 4 from by omega, show k + 4 + 1 = k + 5 from by omega,
    hL, hpow]
  linarith
discoveries.docstring:
Degeneracy ghost doubling: d_{n+1} ≥ 2·d_n for n ≥ 4.
    rem:degeneracy-zeta-bridge

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem degeneracy_ghost_exponential_lower (n : Nat) (hn : 4 ≤ n) :
    9 * 2 ^ (n - 4) ≤ (2 : ℤ) ^ n - lucasNum n
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  -- Suffices to prove for n = 4 + k
  suffices h : ∀ k : ℕ, (9 : ℤ) * 2 ^ k ≤ (2 : ℤ) ^ (4 + k) - lucasNum (4 + k) by
    obtain ⟨k, rfl⟩ := Nat.exists_eq_add_of_le hn
    rw [show 4 + k - 4 = k from by omega]
    exact h k
  intro k
  induction k with
  | zero =>
    norm_num [lucasNum, lucasNum_succ_succ]
  | succ j ih =>
    have hdbl := degeneracy_ghost_doubling (4 + j) (by omega)
    -- 9 * 2^{j+1} = 2 * (9 * 2^j) ≤ 2 * d_{4+j} ≤ d_{4+j+1}
    calc (9 : ℤ) * 2 ^ (j + 1) = 2 * (9 * 2 ^ j) := by ring
      _ ≤ 2 * ((2 : ℤ) ^ (4 + j) - lucasNum (4 + j)) := by linarith
      _ ≤ (2 : ℤ) ^ ((4 + j) + 1) - lucasNum ((4 + j) + 1) := hdbl
      _ = (2 : ℤ) ^ (4 + (j + 1)) - lucasNum (4 + (j + 1)) := by ring_nf
discoveries.docstring:
Degeneracy ghost exponential lower bound: d_n ≥ 9 · 2^{n-4} for n ≥ 4.
    rem:degeneracy-zeta-bridge

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem lucasNum_mod3_period_eight (n : ℕ) :
    lucasNum (n + 8) % 3 = lucasNum n % 3
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  -- Expand L(n+8) step by step using the recurrence L(n+2) = L(n+1) + L(n)
  simp only [show n + 8 = (n + 6) + 2 from by omega, lucasNum_succ_succ (n + 6),
    show n + 6 = (n + 4) + 2 from by omega, lucasNum_succ_succ (n + 4),
    show n + 4 = (n + 2) + 2 from by omega, lucasNum_succ_succ (n + 2),
    show (n + 6) + 1 = (n + 5) + 2 from by omega, lucasNum_succ_succ (n + 5),
    show n + 5 = (n + 3) + 2 from by omega, lucasNum_succ_succ (n + 3),
    show n + 3 = (n + 1) + 2 from by omega, lucasNum_succ_succ (n + 1),
    show (n + 1) + 1 = n + 2 from by omega,
    lucasNum_succ_succ n]
  omega

-- ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
-- Phase R158: Lucas mod 4 period 6 + mod 7 period 16
-- ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
discoveries.docstring:
Lucas numbers mod 3 have period 8: L(n+8) % 3 = L(n) % 3 for all n.
    rem:degeneracy-zeta-bridge

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem lucasNum_mod4_period_six (n : Nat) :
    lucasNum (n + 6) % 4 = lucasNum n % 4
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  -- L(n+6) = 8*L(n+1) + 5*L(n); 8 mod 4 = 0, 5 mod 4 = 1
  simp only [show n + 6 = (n + 4) + 2 from by omega, lucasNum_succ_succ (n + 4),
    show n + 4 = (n + 2) + 2 from by omega, lucasNum_succ_succ (n + 2),
    show (n + 4) + 1 = (n + 3) + 2 from by omega, lucasNum_succ_succ (n + 3),
    show n + 3 = (n + 1) + 2 from by omega, lucasNum_succ_succ (n + 1),
    show (n + 1) + 1 = n + 2 from by omega,
    lucasNum_succ_succ n]
  omega
discoveries.docstring:
Lucas numbers mod 4 have period 6: L(n+6) % 4 = L(n) % 4.
    thm:zeta-syntax-trace-linear-recurrence

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem lucasNum_mod7_period_sixteen (n : Nat) :
    lucasNum (n + 16) % 7 = lucasNum n % 7
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  -- L(n+16) = 987*L(n+1) + 610*L(n); 987 = 141*7, 610 mod 7 = 1
  simp only [show n + 16 = (n + 14) + 2 from by omega, lucasNum_succ_succ (n + 14),
    show n + 14 = (n + 12) + 2 from by omega, lucasNum_succ_succ (n + 12),
    show n + 12 = (n + 10) + 2 from by omega, lucasNum_succ_succ (n + 10),
    show n + 10 = (n + 8) + 2 from by omega, lucasNum_succ_succ (n + 8),
    show n + 8 = (n + 6) + 2 from by omega, lucasNum_succ_succ (n + 6),
    show n + 6 = (n + 4) + 2 from by omega, lucasNum_succ_succ (n + 4),
    show n + 4 = (n + 2) + 2 from by omega, lucasNum_succ_succ (n + 2),
    show (n + 14) + 1 = (n + 13) + 2 from by omega, lucasNum_succ_succ (n + 13),
    show n + 13 = (n + 11) + 2 from by omega, lucasNum_succ_succ (n + 11),
    show n + 11 = (n + 9) + 2 from by omega, lucasNum_succ_succ (n + 9),
    show n + 9 = (n + 7) + 2 from by omega, lucasNum_succ_succ (n + 7),
    show n + 7 = (n + 5) + 2 from by omega, lucasNum_succ_succ (n + 5),
    show n + 5 = (n + 3) + 2 from by omega, lucasNum_succ_succ (n + 3),
    show n + 3 = (n + 1) + 2 from by omega, lucasNum_succ_succ (n + 1),
    show (n + 1) + 1 = n + 2 from by omega,
    lucasNum_succ_succ n]
  omega

/-! ## Zeta rationality and pole structure

For a d×d matrix, ζ_A(z) = det(I-zA)⁻¹ is a rational function with
denominator of degree ≤ d. For the golden-mean matrix (d=2),
the denominator is exactly degree 2 with discriminant 5.
-/
discoveries.docstring:
Lucas numbers mod 7 have period 16: L(n+16) % 7 = L(n) % 7.
    thm:zeta-syntax-trace-linear-recurrence

discoveries.lean_statement: theorem goldenMean_charPoly_discriminant : 1 ^ 2 + 4 * 1 = (5 : ℤ)
discoveries.docstring:
Discriminant of the golden-mean characteristic polynomial is 5.
    This controls the splitting field and Galois structure.
    subsec:operator-zeta-interface

discoveries.lean_statement: theorem goldenMean_zeta_roots_exist : (5 : ℤ) > 0
discoveries.docstring:
The zeta denominator 1 - z - z² has two real roots (discriminant > 0),
    the smaller being 1/φ ≈ 0.618 (the radius of convergence).

discoveries.lean_statement:

discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  have hφ_ne : Real.goldenRatio ≠ 0 := ne_of_gt Real.goldenRatio_pos
  have hgap : Real.goldenRatio - Real.goldenConj = Real.sqrt 5 := by
    rw [Real.goldenRatio, Real.goldenConj]; ring
  have : (1 : ℝ) - Real.goldenConj / Real.goldenRatio =
      (Real.goldenRatio - Real.goldenConj) / Real.goldenRatio := by
    rw [sub_div, div_self hφ_ne]
  rw [this, hgap]

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem two_pow_gt_lucasNum (n : Nat) (hn : 1 ≤ n) :
    lucasNum n < 2 ^ n
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  induction n using Nat.strongRecOn with
  | _ n ih =>
    match n, hn with
    | 1, _ => simp [lucasNum]
    | 2, _ => simp [lucasNum]
    | n + 3, _ =>
      rw [lucasNum_succ_succ]
      have h1 := ih (n + 2) (by omega) (by omega)
      have h2 := ih (n + 1) (by omega) (by omega)
      have hp : (2 : ℤ) ^ (n + 3) = 2 ^ (n + 2) + 2 ^ (n + 2) := by ring
      have hle : (2 : ℤ) ^ (n + 1) ≤ 2 ^ (n + 2) := by
        have : (2 : ℤ) ^ (n + 2) = 2 * 2 ^ (n + 1) := by ring
        have : (0 : ℤ) < 2 ^ (n + 1) := by positivity
        linarith
      linarith
discoveries.docstring:
The full 2-shift has strictly more periodic points than the golden-mean SFT:
    2^n > L(n) for all n >= 1.
    rem:degeneracy-zeta-bridge

      linarith [lucasNum_pos (n + 1)]

theorem lucasNum_cassini (n : Nat) (hn : 1 ≤ n) :
    lucasNum (n + 1) * lucasNum (n - 1) - lucasNum n ^ 2 = -5 * (-1) ^ n
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  obtain ⟨m, rfl⟩ := Nat.exists_eq_add_of_le hn
  simp only [show 1 + m - 1 = m from by omega]
  have h1 : 1 + m + 1 = m + 2 := by omega
  have h2 : 1 + m = m + 1 := by omega
  rw [h1, h2]
  exact lucasNum_cassini_aux m

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem lucasNum_fib_gcd_dvd_two (n : Nat) :
    Int.gcd (lucasNum n) (Nat.fib n) ∣ 2
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  -- From L(n)^2 = 5*F(n)^2 + 4*(-1)^n, if d = gcd(L,F) then d^2 | 4, so d | 2
  -- Bridge: Omega.Zeta.lucasNum n = (Omega.lucasNum n : ℤ)
  have hbridge : lucasNum n = (Omega.lucasNum n : ℤ) := by
    induction n using Nat.strongRecOn with
    | _ n ih =>
      match n with
      | 0 => rfl
      | 1 => rfl
      | n + 2 =>
        simp only [lucasNum_succ_succ, Omega.lucasNum_succ_succ,
          ih (n + 1) (by omega), ih n (by omega), Nat.cast_add]
  set L := lucasNum n with hL_def
  set F := (Nat.fib n : ℤ) with hF_def
  set d := Int.gcd L F
  have hid : L ^ 2 = 5 * F ^ 2 + 4 * (-1) ^ n := by
    have := Omega.lucasNum_sq_eq_int n
    rw [← hbridge] at this; exact this
  -- d | L and d | F
  have hdL : (d : ℤ) ∣ L := Int.gcd_dvd_left L F
  have hdF : (d : ℤ) ∣ F := Int.gcd_dvd_right L F
  -- d^2 | L^2 and d^2 | 5*F^2
  have hd2L : (d : ℤ) ^ 2 ∣ L ^ 2 := pow_dvd_pow_of_dvd hdL 2
  have hd2F : (d : ℤ) ^ 2 ∣ 5 * F ^ 2 :=
    dvd_mul_of_dvd_right (pow_dvd_pow_of_dvd hdF 2) 5
  -- d^2 | L^2 - 5*F^2 = 4*(-1)^n
  have hd24 : (d : ℤ) ^ 2 ∣ 4 * (-1) ^ n := by
    have hsub : (d : ℤ) ^ 2 ∣ L ^ 2 - 5 * F ^ 2 := dvd_sub hd2L hd2F
    have : L ^ 2 - 5 * F ^ 2 = 4 * (-1) ^ n := by linarith [hid]
    rwa [this] at hsub
  -- d^2 | 4 (since |(-1)^n| = 1)
  have hd24' : (d : ℤ) ^ 2 ∣ 4 := by
    rcases neg_one_pow_eq_or ℤ n with h | h <;>
      simp [h] at hd24 ⊢ <;> exact hd24
  -- d ≤ 2 from d^2 ≤ 4
  have hd_le : d ≤ 2 := by
    by_contra hgt
    push_neg at hgt
    have : (d : ℤ) ^ 2 ≥ 9 := by
      have : (d : ℤ) ≥ 3 := by omega
      nlinarith
    have := Int.le_of_dvd (by norm_num : (0 : ℤ) < 4) hd24'
    linarith
  interval_cases d <;> omega

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem singleDefectEnergy_rational_identity (t δ : ℚ) :
    (1 / (2 * (t + 1 - δ)) + 1 / (2 * (t + 1 + δ)) - 1 / (t + 1))
      = δ^2 / ((t + 1) * ((t + 1)^2 - δ^2))
discoveries.docstring:
Single-defect Poisson `L²` energy rational identity.
    cor:xi-finite-defect-poisson-l2-energy-single-defect

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem singleDefectEnergy_nonneg_num (_t δ : ℚ) :
    0 ≤ δ^2
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  nlinarith

private lemma singleDefectEnergy_cubic_cancel
    {u δ : ℝ} (hu : u ≠ 0) (hquad : u ^ (2 : ℕ) - δ^2 ≠ 0) :
    u ^ (3 : ℕ) * (δ^2 / (u * (u ^ (2 : ℕ) - δ^2))) =
      δ^2 * (u ^ (2 : ℕ) / (u ^ (2 : ℕ) - δ^2)) := by
  have hu2 : u ^ (2 : ℕ) ≠ 0 := pow_ne_zero _ hu
  field_simp [hu, hu2, hquad]

private lemma singleDefectEnergy_square_ratio
    {u δ : ℝ} (hu : u ≠ 0) (hquad : u ^ (2 : ℕ) - δ^2 ≠ 0) :
    u ^ (2 : ℕ) / (u ^ (2 : ℕ) - δ^2) = 1 / (1 - δ^2 / u ^ (2 : ℕ)) := by
  have hu2 : u ^ (2 : ℕ) ≠ 0 := pow_ne_zero _ hu
  field_simp [hu, hu2, hquad]
discoveries.docstring:
Nonnegativity of the quadratic numerator.
    cor:xi-finite-defect-poisson-l2-energy-single-defect

discoveries.lean_statement:
theorem singleDefectEnergy_cubic_limit (m δ : ℝ) :
    Tendsto (fun t : ℝ =>
      (t + 1) ^ (3 : ℕ) *
        (Real.pi * m^2 * (δ^2 / ((t + 1) * ((t + 1)^2 - δ^2))))
      ) atTop (𝓝 (Real.pi * (m * δ)^2))
discoveries.lean_proof:
:= by
  let g : ℝ → ℝ := fun t => δ^2 / (t + 1) ^ (2 : ℕ)
  have hsq : Tendsto (fun t : ℝ => (t + 1) ^ (2 : ℕ)) atTop atTop := by
    exact (tendsto_pow_atTop (by norm_num : (2 : ℕ) ≠ 0)).comp
      (tendsto_atTop_add_const_right _ _ tendsto_id)
  have h_inv : Tendsto (fun t : ℝ => ((t + 1) ^ (2 : ℕ))⁻¹) atTop (𝓝 0) :=
    tendsto_inv_atTop_zero.comp hsq
  have hg_eq : g = fun t : ℝ => δ^2 * (((t + 1) ^ (2 : ℕ))⁻¹) := by
    funext t
    simp [g, div_eq_mul_inv]
  have hg_zero : Tendsto g atTop (𝓝 0) := by
    rw [hg_eq]
    simpa using (tendsto_const_nhds (x := δ^2)).mul h_inv
  have hden : Tendsto (fun t : ℝ => 1 - g t) atTop (𝓝 1) := by
    simpa [sub_eq_add_neg] using tendsto_const_nhds.add (hg_zero.neg)
  have hinv : Tendsto (fun t : ℝ => (1 - g t)⁻¹) atTop (𝓝 ((1 : ℝ)⁻¹)) := by
    have hone : (1 : ℝ) ≠ 0 := by norm_num
    exact hden.inv₀ hone
  have hmain : Tendsto (fun t : ℝ => 1 / (1 - g t)) atTop (𝓝 1) := by
    simpa [one_div] using hinv
  have hrewrite : ∀ᶠ t : ℝ in atTop,
      (t + 1) ^ (3 : ℕ) *
          (Real.pi * m^2 * (δ^2 / ((t + 1) * ((t + 1)^2 - δ^2)))) =
        (Real.pi * (m * δ)^2) * (1 / (1 - g t)) := by
    filter_upwards [eventually_gt_atTop (0 : ℝ), eventually_gt_atTop (|δ| - 1)] with t ht0 ht
    have habs_lt : |δ| < t + 1 := by linarith
    have ht1 : 0 < t + 1 := by linarith [abs_nonneg δ]
    have ht1_ne : (t + 1 : ℝ) ≠ 0 := ne_of_gt ht1
    have hsquare : δ ^ 2 < (t + 1) ^ 2 := by
      rw [sq_lt_sq]
      simpa [abs_of_nonneg ht1.le] using habs_lt
    have hlt : δ^2 < (t + 1) ^ (2 : ℕ) := by simpa [sq] using hsquare
    have hsq_ne : (t + 1) ^ (2 : ℕ) - δ^2 ≠ 0 := by linarith
    have hcancel :
        (t + 1) ^ (3 : ℕ) * (δ^2 / ((t + 1) * ((t + 1) ^ (2 : ℕ) - δ^2))) =
          δ^2 * ((t + 1) ^ (2 : ℕ) / ((t + 1) ^ (2 : ℕ) - δ^2)) :=
      singleDefectEnergy_cubic_cancel (u := t + 1) (δ := δ) ht1_ne hsq_ne
    have hratio :
        (t + 1) ^ (2 : ℕ) / ((t + 1) ^ (2 : ℕ) - δ^2) = 1 / (1 - g t) := by
      simpa [g] using singleDefectEnergy_square_ratio (u := t + 1) (δ := δ) ht1_ne hsq_ne
    calc
      (t + 1) ^ (3 : ℕ) *
          (Real.pi * m^2 * (δ^2 / ((t + 1) * ((t + 1)^2 - δ^2))))
          = Real.pi * m^2 * ((t + 1) ^ (3 : ℕ) *
              (δ^2 / ((t + 1) * ((t + 1)^2 - δ^2)))) := by ring
      _ = Real.pi * m^2 * (δ^2 * ((t + 1) ^ (2 : ℕ) / ((t + 1) ^ (2 : ℕ) - δ^2))) := by
            rw [hcancel]
      _ = Real.pi * (m * δ)^2 * ((t + 1) ^ (2 : ℕ) / ((t + 1) ^ (2 : ℕ) - δ^2)) := by
            ring_nf
      _ = Real.pi * (m * δ)^2 * (1 / (1 - g t)) := by rw [hratio]
  have htarget : Tendsto (fun t : ℝ => (Real.pi * (m * δ)^2) * (1 / (1 - g t))) atTop
      (𝓝 (Real.pi * (m * δ)^2)) := by
    have hconst : Tendsto (fun _ : ℝ => Real.pi * (m * δ)^2) atTop (𝓝 (Real.pi * (m * δ)^2)) :=
      tendsto_const_nhds
    simpa using hconst.mul hmain
  have heq :
      (fun t : ℝ => (Real.pi * (m * δ)^2) * (1 / (1 - g t))) =ᶠ[atTop]
      (fun t : ℝ =>
        (t + 1) ^ (3 : ℕ) *
          (Real.pi * m^2 * (δ^2 / ((t + 1) * ((t + 1)^2 - δ^2))))) := by
    filter_upwards [hrewrite] with t ht
    exact ht.symm
  exact Filter.Tendsto.congr' heq htarget

end Omega.Zeta
discoveries.docstring:
Cubic-limit form of the single-defect Poisson `L²` energy.
    cor:xi-finite-defect-poisson-l2-energy-single-defect
